title: Retainers
copy: Retainers
Retainers provide the final touch to your orthodontic journey, securing your results. Schedule an appointment in London online today.
How we can help you?

Retainers are specialised orthodontic devices used to save the results after completing treatment with braces or aligners. Teeth naturally have a propensity to return to their original position even after they were levelled. Retainers assist "lock in" the results that have been achieved, by maintaining the teeth in their correct alignment and preventing any undesired movement.

Because it takes time for the tissues around the teeth to adjust to their new placements, retainers are necessary for maintaining the results of therapy. In the absence of retainers, there is a considerable chance that teeth may revert to their original position. In order to get a beautiful smile and long-term oral health, they are an essential last step in orthodontic treatment. We are waiting for you at our London clinic.
Retainer Types at Our Clinic
At KClinics, we offers two types of retainers to maintain the results of your orthodontic treatment:
- Removable retainer: a transparent, user-friendly aligner that is perfect for wearing at night. - Fixed retainer: a thin metal wire affixed to the inside of the teeth for long-term stability.
Our experts will assist you in choosing the best type of retainers.
The Cost of Retainers
At our clinic, the cost of a consultation is £50, which is taken as a deposit. This deposit can be fully used towards any future treatments you decide to proceed with.
[wbcr_php_snippet id="28173"]
Bonded Retainers (Permanent or Fixed Retainers)

Bonded retainers, also known as permanent retainers, are tiny wires that are adhered with a specialised dental glue to the inside of the front teeth, often from canine to canine. This wire helps to maintain the correct position of the teeth, giving them stable support. Bonded retainers work continuously, stabilising the teeth and preventing any movement after braces treatment.
Benefits of Permanent Retainers
The permanent retainer doesn’t need to be removed, ensuring constant support for the front teeth, which are the most prone to relapse after treatment.
When speaking or smiling, the wire is undetectable since it is affixed to the inside of the teeth.
After installation, patients rarely perceive the retainer on their teeth, and it becomes completely unnoticeable over time.
How Long Should You Wear a Permanent Retainer?
Removable retainers

Removable retainers are transparent orthodontic trays that cover the entire dental arch, holding the teeth in the correct position after completing braces treatment. Because retainers are manufactured specifically for each patient using exact moulds or 3D scans, they fit comfortably and effectively sustain the results that have been obtained.

Removable retainers are usually composed of thin, translucent plastic and are almost invisible on teeth. They are easy to remove for eating, brushing and other hygiene routines, which is the biggest advantage for patients.
Benefits of Removable Retainers
Detachable retainers are a visually appealing alternative since they are made of clear plastic and almost invisible on the teeth.
It is easy to take out removable retainers for brushing, flossing and other oral hygiene procedures.
Since each retainer is created to every patient`s, the teeth will fit comfortably and precisely.
How Long Should You Wear a Removable Retainer?
The period of time a person wears removable retainers is determined by their own needs as well as the advice of their orthodontist. Patients typically wear them:
For the first 3-6 months — full-time, except while eating and for hygiene routines.
After this period — usually only at night to maintain the treatment results over the long term.
Orthodontists frequently recommend wearing removable retainers at night for several years, and sometimes even for life, to prevent any potential tooth shifting.
How to Choose the Right Retainer?

An orthodontist recommended you the best option when deciding between a permanent and removable retainer, since both may be suggested in tandem for best outcomes. In order to stabilise the front teeth, orthodontists frequently combine the use of a permanent retainer with a removable retainer to support the complete dental arch.
Retainers are an important last step in orthodontic treatment, regardless of the type that works best for you. They will help you maintain a gorgeous, straight smile for many years to come.
Caring for Removable Retainers
Following the right care instructions is crucial to preserving the hygienic conditions and clarity of removable retainers:

Frequent Cleaning: To prevent scratching, carefully brush the retainer with a soft toothbrush devoid of toothpaste and rinse it with warm water after every removal. Additionally, you may use specific retainer cleaning solutions.
Storage in a Case: To avoid some contamination or damage, the retainer should be kept in a protective case when not in use.
Avoid Hot Water: Only mild or cold water should be used to clean the retainer since hot water might distort the plastic.
[elementor-template id="9210"]
Frequently Asked Questions

Yes, the majority of people adjust to retainers rather fast. While detachable retainers are simple to take off and put back on, making eating and hygiene easier, permanent retainers are undetectable and do not obstruct speaking.

Because permanent retainers are affixed to the inner surfaces of the teeth, they are imperceptible. Removable retainers are nearly undetectable on teeth because they are composed of transparent plastic.
Long-term retainers: To avoid plaque accumulation, it’s important to use dental floss or interdental brushes to clean the teeth around the wire on a regular basis.
Detachable retainers: After removal, gently brush retainers without toothpaste, use only warm water and preserve in a safe container.

Permanent retainers can shatter or separate from the teeth, requiring repair. Removable retainers should be maintained in a case and away from hot water since they can distort in hot temperatures or break if improperly stored.
Every 6-12 months, we recommend visiting your orthodontist for a retainer check-up. This makes it possible to identify any problems and fix them in order to preserve consistent outcomes.

Inform your orthodontist immediately if a permanent retainer breaks or a detachable retainer disappears. Wearing a retainer is essential to maintaining treatment results, so replacement or repair should be done promptly.
When you first start wearing a retainer, some minor discomfort is expected, but any pain should go away in a few days. See your orthodontist if it continues.
Yes, after a few years, removable retainers may need to be replaced due to wear and tear. Permanent retainers can also need to be replaced if they deteriorate or break.
Yes, you should inform your orthodontist right away to set up a replacement if you lose your retainer.
Without a retainer, teeth can start moving in a matter of days or weeks, so it’s critical to wear it consistently and adhere to your orthodontist’s recommendations.

title: Aesthetic Dentistry
copy: Aesthetic Dentistry

Aesthetic dentistry is a set of treatments to improve the teeth's health and appearance. It includes implants, prosthetics, teeth straightening, whitening and other cosmetic options like veneers and composite bonding. Our expert care ensures you a gorgeous result and confidence in smile.
[wbcr_php_snippet id="24505"]
Consultation and Preparation for Implantation

Consultation and preparation for implantation is the first important step toward restoring your smile. Our doctors perform diagnostics, evaluate the state of your oral cavity and go over each case detail during the consultation. WWe will explain all stages of the procedure, answer your questions, and help you choose the best treatment option. Preparation includes thorough planning, carrying out any required diagnostic tests, such as CT or X-rays, and, if required, prepping the bone tissue. Every step is executed with the highest level of precision and consideration for your comfort.
Dental Implant Placement

Dental implant placement is a modern and reliable way to restore missing teeth. This process ensures comfort and longevity while restoring your smile's natural look and functioning. An implant composed of biocompatible titanium is surgically inserted into the jawbone during the procedure. The process is performed under local anesthesia, so you won’t feel any pain. A strong foundation for a future crown or bridge is then created when the implant fuses with the bone tissue.
Crown Fabrication and Placement

Crown fabrication and placement is an effective way to restore a damaged or decayed tooth. In addition to protecting the compromised tooth, modern crowns provide your smile a natural look and feel. In order to provide a foundation for the crown, the dentist first prepares the tooth by cleaning it of damaged tissues and shaping it naturally. Impressions are then taken, which are used to craft a custom crown that perfectly matches the shape, size and color of your tooth. Then an individually made crown is securely placed on the tooth, ensuring durability and comfort.
Bridge Fabrication and Placement

Bridge fabrication and placement is a reliable method to restore one or more missing teeth. In addition to giving your smile a beautiful appearance, a dental bridge fully restores its functionality, allowing you to chew and speak comfortably. The first step in the procedure is to prepare the supporting teeth that will act as the bridge's anchors. After that, measurements are made to design a unique bridge that perfectly matches your anatomy. The bridge is crafted from high-quality materials to ensure durability and a natural look. The bridge's secure placement on the prepared teeth or implants provide stability and comfort.
Dentures and Implant-Supported Dentures

Dentures and implant-supported dentures are effective solutions for restoring missing teeth and improving your smile. You can choose dentures that are detachable or that are fixed to implants for more comfort and stability, according to your preferences. Each patient's removable dentures are manufactured specifically for them, taking into account the unique characteristics and form of their oral cavity. Implant-supported dentures are fixed on implants, providing a more natural look and feel in conjunction with enhanced durability. Regardless of the type you choose, our specialists ensure precise fabrication and comfortable placement to restore your confidence and quality of life.
Braces

Braces are an effective and proven method for straightening teeth and correcting bite issues. They help produce a beautiful smile, enhance the appearance and functioning of your teeth and promote general oral health. Braces are then fitted to gently shift your teeth into the ideal position using controlled pressure. We provide a variety of brace types, including ceramic and metal options for your needs and preferences.
Aligners

Aligners are a modern and convenient way to straighten teeth, which is becoming increasingly popular due to their aesthetic appeal and comfort. Aligners are virtually invisible due to the fact that they are made of transparent material, making them an ideal choice for those who want to straighten your teeth without traditional braces. Aligners are a perfect choice to achieve an ideal smile while maintaining a natural appearance throughout the treatment process.
Retainers

Retainers are an important step in maintaining the desired results after finishing orthodontic treatment. They keep teeth from moving back and stabilise them in their new places. Custom-made retainers offer a precise fit and comfort. At KClinics available both options are available, fixed and removable retainers, depending on your needs and your orthodontist's advice. Regular use of retainers is key to preserving your smile after braces or aligners.
Veneers

Veneers are an aesthetic and quick solution for achieving the perfect smile. These are thin shells made from high-quality ceramic, bonded to the front surface of the teeth to improve their appearance. Veneers provide a natural appearance and long-lasting results by successfully hiding enamel flaws, fissures, unevenness or discolouration. You may have the smile of your dreams quickly and painlessly with this method.
Composite Bonding

Composite bonding is a contemporary and adaptable technique that enables you to quickly enhance the look of your teeth. Using a unique composite material that matches the color of your teeth, we can fix discolouration, chips, fractures, and uneven edges. Composite bonding is a fast, affordable, and effective way to restore the aesthetics of your smile and boost your confidence.
Read more
Teeth Whitening

Your smile may be brightened quickly and safely with the procedure teeth whitening. The procedure effectively removes stains and discoloration caused by coffee, tea, tobacco, or natural enamel aging. We give two whitening options: at-home whitening, which uses whitening gel and custom trays to ensure progressive improvement, and professional in-office treatment, which uses innovative gels and technology to produce instant results.
Smile Enhancement

Smile enhancement is a combination of procedures designed to improve the appearance of your smile. These treatments address problems like tooth color, shape, alignment and overall symmetry. Popular choices on your way to smile are composite bonding, veneers, orthodontics and teeth whitening. And a personalised approach ensures a natural and harmonious smile that boosts your confidence every day. Start your journey to a perfect smile today!
[elementor-template id="9210"]

title: Veneers
copy: Veneers
Transform your look with veneers designed to bring out the best in your teeth. Schedule an appointment in London online today.
How we can help you?

The dream of a perfectly white, flawless smile can become a reality with porcelain veneers. With the use of these thin medical porcelain coatings, modern dentistry not only restores the natural beauty of your smile but also creates a faultless look. Veneers provide teeth a healthy and natural appearance by concealing flaws including stains, fissures, uneven edges or spaces between teeth. Porcelain veneers are a popular option for those who wish to feel more confident about their smile because of its stain resistance and durability. In addition to improving appearance, this process can prevent the need for time-consuming and lengthy orthodontic or surgical procedures. We are waiting for you at our London clinic.
Who Are Suitable for Veneers?
Veneers made of porcelain are perfect for those who want to:
Achieve a bright and lasting tooth colour that won’t change over time
Cover up obvious flaws like chips, cracks, or jagged edges.
Adjust the alignment and shape of teeth without orthodontic treatment.
Close the spaces between teeth for a harmonic and visually appealing smile.
The Cost of Veneers
At our clinic, the cost of a consultation is £50, which is taken as a deposit. This deposit can be fully used towards any future treatments you decide to proceed with.
from £1500
How Are Porcelain Veneers Applied?
First consultation
During the appointment the dentist looks at your teeth, discusses with you about your wants and objectives, and assists you in choosing the perfect veneer colour and form.
Tooth Preparation
To provide space for the veneer, a small layer of enamel, typically 0.3–0.7 mm, is removed from the tooth surface.
Taking Impressions
After preparation, the dentist takes impressions of your teeth, which are sent to a laboratory to create custom veneers.
Temporary Veneers
A temporary bridge may be affixed to safeguard the prepared teeth, while the permanent bridge is being created.
Application
Once fabricated, the porcelain veneers are carefully tested and adjusted for a perfect fit, then bonded with a special dental adhesive that securely holds the veneers in place.
[wbcr_php_snippet id="10756"]
What Are Porcelain Veneers Made Of?
Premium medical porcelain that has been carefully created for dental purposes serves as the main component of these veneers. The features of this porcelain include:
Natural Look and Shine: Because porcelain replicates teeth's natural sheen, veneers are almost indistinguishable from natural teeth.
Stain Resistance: Porcelain is more stain-resistant than natural enamel.
Durability: Porcelain is a durable and resistant to wear material, so is therefore for long-term usage.
How Long Do Porcelain Veneers Last?

Porcelain veneers usually last 10 to 15 years when properly maintained. If the patient complies with their dentist's advice, veneers may sometimes last even longer. Even though porcelain is resistant to wear and damage, in order to prolong the veneers' lifespan, it is important to prevent severe impacts or excessive strain on the teeth.
[elementor-template id="9210"]
How to Care for Porcelain Veneers?
Frequent Tooth Brushing: Clean your teeth twice a day by using a soft toothbrush and non-abrasive toothpaste too.
Avoid Hard Foods: To avoid some chipping, try not to chew on hard foods like nuts, sweets, etc.
Frequent dental checkups: Frequent check-ups enable the dentist to monitor the veneers' condition and detect any potential issues early.
Use Dental Floss: Clean between teeth with floss to maintain gum health and prevent bacteria buildup.
Frequently Asked Questions
The veneer placement procedure is rather painless because before the procedure we use anaesthetic. Although there may be some little pain, most people find the process to be comfortable.

Veneers typically take 2-3 dentist appointments for installation. Consultation and preparation take place during the first appointment, impressions are taken during the second and the veneers are finally placed during the third.
Veneers are permanent and cannot be taken out. But when they are broken, the dentist can remove them. However, depending on care and tooth health, they may need replacement after 10-15 years.
Yes, if veneers are exposed to extremely hard foods or excessive force, they may crack or shatter. See your dentist about replacing or repairing a broken veneer.
Because porcelain is so stain-resistant, veneers will stay coloured for a lot longer than natural enamel—as long as they are properly maintained and exposed to less staining agents.
Most individuals can have veneers, but if you have serious dental problems like gum inflammation or widespread tooth decay, your dentist could suggest alternative treatments first.
In order to create a more natural look and beautiful smile, porcelain veneers can indeed assist bridge small gaps between teeth.
Since porcelain cannot be whitened, it is crucial to take care of it properly and stay away from staining agents. If colour change is necessary over time, the veneers will need to be replaced.
In order to enhance the appearance of a smile, porcelain veneers are usually applied to the front teeth. If required, they can occasionally be positioned on side teeth.
After veneers are installed, there may be a little sensitivity to hot or cold meals, but this normally goes away in a few days.
Although veneers are often not applied to teeth that have crowns, your dentist may recommend other cosmetic restoration techniques.

title: Composite bonding
copy: Composite bonding
Composite bonding provides a quick, effective way to fix minor imperfections in your teeth. Schedule an appointment in London online today.
How we can help you?

Composite bonding (or cosmetic tooth restoration) is a cosmetic dental method that quickly and effectively corrects various tooth imperfections, providing a natural and aesthetically pleasing smile. Through the application of a unique composite substance to the tooth surface, an optimum contour is created, allowing for the adjustment of tooth colour, shape and texture. For people who want fast outcomes without requiring lengthy procedures and substantial interventions, composite bonding is ideal. It is a popular option for many people since significant changes may frequently be made in a single dental appointment. We are waiting for you at our London clinic.
Who is Composite Bonding Suitable For?
Composite bonding is appropriate for individuals who want to:
Repair small cosmetic defects like stains, chips or fractures.
To create a more harmonious smile adjust the length or contour of your teeth.
Fill up the little spaces between your teeth.
repair a tooth that has been damaged or decayed.
The Cost Composite Bonding
At our clinic, the cost of a consultation is £50, which is taken as a deposit. This deposit can be fully used towards any future treatments you decide to proceed with.
from £250
Process stages of composite bonding
Tooth Preparation
The dentist cleans the tooth and can, if required, lightly treat its surface to improve adherence with the composite material.
Applying the Composite
The tooth is covered with a layer of composite resin that has been meticulously matched to your natural tooth's colour.
Forming the Material
To replicate the tooth's natural appearance, the dentist forms the composite to give it the proper shape and size.
Polymerization
The composite is fastened to the tooth by hardening under a specific light.
Smoothing and Polishing
The last stage the composite is polished to a sheen and smooth surface that matches the natural teeth..
[wbcr_php_snippet id="10756"]
Benefits of Composite Bonding
Speed : Depending on the tooth the process typically takes 30 to 60 minutes.
Minimal Invasiveness : The natural enamel is preserved by requiring little to no teeth preparation.
Affordability : Composite bonding is less expensive than crowns or veneers.
Natural seem : The teeth seem natural even with colour and form adjustment.
How Long Does Composite Bonding Last?

Depending on usage and maintenance, composite bonding can last from five to ten years. Avoiding excessive strain on the teeth (e.g., opening packages or chewing hard objects) is essential, as the composite material may get damaged or lose its integrity.
[elementor-template id="9210"]
How to Care for Teeth with Composite Bonding?
These easy care instructions can help your composite bonding last as long as possible and keep natural appearance:

Regular Tooth Brushing : To avoid composite wear, clean your teeth twice a day with a soft toothbrush and non-abrasive toothpaste. The composite surface may be scratched by abrasive materials, increasing the staining potential.
Mouthwash and Dental Floss : Use mouthwash and dental floss to keep your gums healthy and to get food particles out between teeth.
Avoid Staining Products : Avoid items that might discolour the composite material, like coffee, tea, red wine and other, because it is more prone to staining than natural enamel or porcelain.
Limit Hard Foods : Hard foods, including hard candies or nuts, might cause the composite to chip or break. Avoid applying too much pressure to teeth that have had bonding done.
Dental Visits : Regular checkups helps in time identify any problems with the composite and offer expert polishing to maintain its shine and stain resistance.
Frequently Asked Questions

In most cases, composite bonding is painless. Since the technique only requires minimum tooth surface preparation, anaesthesia is not necessary. To provide comfort, a mild anaesthetic may be used if significant reshaping is required.
Composite bonding works well, but it is not as strong as porcelain veneers and can crack or discolour easily. Over time, it might need to be replaced or touch-up sometimes.

Yes, composite resin is more prone to discoloration. Coffee, tea, red wine, smoking and other staining agents can cause bonding to discolour over time. Discoloration can be avoided by practising good oral hygiene and using staining agents sparingly.
Unlike real teeth, composite bonding is not affected by teeth whitening. Your dentist can update or replace the bonding if the material’s colour changes over time.
Yes, small damage to composite bonding may be fixed. If necessary, the dentist might replace the composite entirely or apply a fresh coating on some teeth.

Because technique necessitates only minor alterations to the tooth structure, composite bonding is indeed reversible. The composite may be taken out if needed without causing any harm to the tooth below.
Composite bonding is suitable for closing small gaps and making minor cosmetic corrections. Orthodontics or other restorative techniques may be necessary for significant gaps or alignment problems.

Bonding is usually employed for front teeth since the composite material may not be able to tolerate strong chewing pressures. It can, however, occasionally be used on molars for little fixes or cosmetic adjustments.

Because of the additional layers, the treated tooth may feel a little different after bonding. But in a few days the bonding feels like a natural part of the tooth and most patients quickly get used to it.
Yes, veneers can be used in place of composite bonding if preferred. Depending on your cosmetic preferences and the state of your teeth, your dentist can go over your alternatives.

title: Teeth whitening
copy: Teeth whitening
Restore your teeth’s natural brilliance with our professional whitening options. Schedule an appointment in London online today.
How we can help you?

Teeth whitening is one of the most popular procedures among the clients in cosmetic dentistry. Lightening dental enamel and eliminating stains brought on by food, drink, smoking, or ageing naturally are its main objectives. The process gives you a gorgeous smile that greatly increases your self-confidence by restoring or brightening your teeth to their original whiteness. We are waiting for you at our London clinic.
Who is Teeth Whitening Suitable for?

Teeth Whitening is appropriate for individuals who want to: Eliminate stains brought on by smoke, alcohol, tea or coffee. Lighten the natural colour of their teeth. Get ready for important events (e.g., weddings or photoshoots). restore their teeth’s visual attractiveness following years of age or wear. However, we do not recommend teeth whitening for individuals with gum disease, multiple cavities or for pregnant women.
The Cost of Teeth Whitening
At our clinic, the cost of a consultation is £50, which is taken as a deposit. This deposit can be fully used towards any future treatments you decide to proceed with.
from £495
from £650
[wbcr_php_snippet id="27875"]
Professional Whitening teeth

Under a dentist's close supervision, this is the fastest and most efficient method of tooth whitening. High-concentration whitening gels are utilised throughout the process, and they are accelerated by special lights or lasers. Teeth become 5 to 10 shades lighter after the procedure . Because of the expert approach, this technique is secure and reduces gum irritation, and ensures even whitening.
How Does the Procedure Work?
Examination and Consultation
During the first consultation the dentist analyses the state of your teeth and gums, decides whether teeth whitening is appropriate and, if required, cures cavities or inflammation.
Preparation and gums protection
By professional cleaning plaque and tartar. To prevent irritation during the procedure a special gel is applied to the gums.
Whitening Gel Application
The teeth are treated with a high-concentration whitening gel, the concentration of which is tailored to each patient’s teeth.
Gel Activation
The gel’s activity is accelerated by the application of a heat special lamp.
Evaluation of Results and Rinsing
The dentist analyses the colour of your teeth before and after the whitening gel is removed.
[wbcr_php_snippet id="10756"]
At-Home Teeth Whitening

In this method of teeth whitening the patient wears custom-made trays with whitening gel at home. To ensure a flawless fit, the trays are made using impressions of the patient's teeth. The gel progressively lightens teeth by 4 to 8 shades, although its concentration is lower than that of at clinic whitening. The trays are used every day for 1-2 hours or overnight over the 7-14 days treatment. With the right maintenance, this type of teeth whitening is easy to use, effective and provides long-lasting effects.
Consultation and Impressions
Impressions: During the first consultation the dentist takes individual impressions of the teeth, which are sent to a lab to create custom-fitted trays that perfectly fit the patient’s teeth.
Whitening Kit Provision
In addition to the trays, the patient is given instructions on how to use the trays and apply the gel, which contains an active component (often carbamide peroxide).
Use of the Daily Tray
Depending on the dentist's advice, the gel-containing trays are worn every day for one to two hours or overnight to enable gradual and even teeth whitening.
Follow-Up Visits
During follow-up visits, the dentist assesses the patient's progress and modifies the treatment plan as needed.
[wbcr_php_snippet id="12415"]
How Does Teeth Whitening Work?

This method is based on the use of certain active chemicals, including carbamide peroxide or hydrogen peroxide. These compounds by penetrating the enamel and dentin, break down the chemicals that cause tooth discolouration. The best way to deal with the problem of discoloration is to Clinic Whitening Teeth and At-Home whitening. As a result teeth become visible brighter and more evenly coloured.

How to Care after Teeth Whitening?
Use dental floss and fluoride toothpaste to brush your teeth twice a day. We also recommend mouth rinses, because it can support the preservation of freshness and hygiene.
Teeth are most vulnerable to discoloration in the first 48 hours following teeth whitening. Steer clear of smoke, red wine, tea, coffee, and sauces.
This lessens the amount of time that staining liquids come into contact with your teeth.
Seeing your dentist will help you keep an eye on the state of your teeth and keep them looking their best.

Usually, teeth whitening procedures are painful. Temporary tooth sensitivity may occur in certain patients, although it usually goes away soon.

Reduce how long you wear the trays or see your dentist if your sensitivity worsens. They might suggest a lower-concentration gel or a toothpaste designed to reduce sensitivity.
Yes, the dentist keeps an eye on things and modifies the treatment plan as necessary.
Yes, you can repeat the procedure, but only as often as your dentist recommends or no more than once a year.

Before the whitening procedure a professional cleaning is advised, it improves the penetration of the whitening gel by removing tartar and plaque.
In-office whitening can lighten teeth by 5–10 shades, while at-home whitening can achieve 4–8 shades depending on the condition of the enamel and the method used.

About the service

Laser skin rejuvenation is a modern, non-invasive procedure that uses high-energy light to improve the texture, tone and overall condition of the skin. The laser promotes collagen synthesis and natural regeneration processes by penetrating the skin's deep layers. We are waiting for you at our London clinic.

How does the procedure work?

Laser rejuvenation produces the process of tissue coagulation or ablation. This implies that fresh, healthy cells replace ageing, damaged skin. Depending on the program selected, the laser beam targets pigments, capillaries, or collagen specifically while leaving the surrounding tissue unharmed. Additionally, the technique improves blood circulation, which leaves the skin moisturised, smooth and glowing from the first session.
Laser therapy promotes an even skin tone and lessens discolouration for a more radiant complexion by addressing excess pigmentation, UV damage and age spots.
By increasing the production of collagen and elastin, laser skin rejuvenation improves skin moisture and produces firmer, plumper skin with a natural glow.
By promoting collagen synthesis and cellular renewal, the technique successfully minimise fine lines and wrinkles, leaving the skin smoother and younger-looking.
By promoting skin healing, smoothing out uneven areas, and enhancing skin clarity and texture, laser rejuvenation reduces the visibility of acne scars and blemishes.
The skin becomes softer and more vibrant after this treatment, which also minimise pore size and smoothes rough skin.
The laser's heat encourages collagen remodelling, which tightens loose or sagging skin and increases its suppleness.
Laser therapy can lessen redness brought on by rosacea, dilated capillaries or other vascular issues.

About the service

The pigmentation correction procedure uses high-intensity light or laser energy to break down excess pigment on the skin with a higher concentration of melanin. This heat from the conversion of light energy takes out extra pigment from skin without damaging the surrounding tissue. As a result, skin returns natural shine, balances the skin tone and promotes skin cell renewal. We are waiting for you at our London clinic.

How does the procedure work?

The melanin that causes pigmentation spots is targeted by a laser or other strong light that enters the skin during the pigmentation repair process. The pigment is broken down by the light energy's conversion to heat and the body then naturally gets rid of it. Concurrently, the generation of collagen on the skin is boosted, enhancing the structure of the skin and making it firmer, smoother and brighter. The skin's colour and texture balance out after the first session, and pigmentation spots gradually fade or lighten over time.
The process gives dull, uneven skin life and brightness by increasing blood circulation and encouraging cellular turnover.

About the service

The vascular lesions treatment uses high-intensity light or laser energy to target affected blood vessels. Heat is produced by the conversion of light energy, which causes dilated capillaries or blood vessels to coagulate (close) without causing damage to the surrounding tissues. As a result, the vessels are destroyed and the body naturally eliminates them, giving the skin its natural appearance, suppleness and even tone. We are waiting for you at our London clinic.

How does the procedure work?

During the procedure, the laser or intense light penetrates the upper layer of the skin, focusing on the haemoglobin contained in the blood vessels. When heat is produced from the light energy, dilated capillaries shut or become less visible. Simultaneously, collagen synthesis is boosted, resulting in improved skin texture. After just one session, vascular lesions become less noticeable, and the skin appears healthier and more even.
The process promotes cellular regeneration, increases blood circulation, and leaves the skin looking naturally glowing.

It is a modern method for skin tightening without surgery.The deeper layers of the skin are heated by radiofrequency energy throughout the procedure, which promotes the skin's natural rejuvenation process. This promotes the production of collagen and elastin, two vital elements that give skin its firmness and suppleness. As a result your skin gets smoother, fresher and younger. How does the procedure work?

During the RF Lifting procedure, a special device delivers radiofrequency energy into the deeper layers of the skin, ensuring uniform heating of the tissues. This procedure increases blood flow, tightens facial contours, smoothes wrinkles, and promotes the synthesis of collagen and elastin. After just one session, you can notice smoother, firmer and more lifted skin, while a full course of procedures ensures long-lasting results that can last up to a year.

About the service

Do you want to get back the firmness and youth of your skin without surgery? SMAS (HIFU) lifting is a modern ultrasound skin tightening technique that works on the deeper layers of tissue. High-intensity focused ultrasound (HIFU) stimulates the formation of collagen and elastin throughout the procedure, promoting natural rejuvenation processes. These proteins are necessary for the suppleness and hardness of skin. After just one session your skin gets firmer, smoother and more defined contours. We are waiting for you at our London clinic.
Benefits SMAS (HIFU) Lifting
Deep Skin Tightening
Wrinkles & Fine Lines
Facial Contouring
Elasticity of the Skin
Rejuvenation
How does the procedure work?

During the procedure SMAS lifting a specialised tool applies ultrasonic energy to the SMAS layer (superficial muscular aponeurotic system), which is situated deep beneath the skin. This guarantees accurate heating of the tissue, which causes contraction and the synthesis of new collagen. This procedure enhances skin tone, smoothes wrinkles, tightens and elevates face features, and produces a natural rejuvenating effect. You will see tighter, smoother skin with more defined face features after only one session. A complete course provides long-lasting results that can last up to 18 months.
Face SMAS Lifting provides long-lasting tightening and lifting for better face shapes by focusing on deeper layers of the skin and muscles.
By promoting the creation of collagen in the deeper layers of the skin, the technique successfully minimises wrinkles and smoothes fine lines.
SMAS Lifting helps the face look more young and sculpted by defining and enhancing face features.
The treatment restores skin elasticity by rejuvenating the deeper structural layers, improving firmness and resilience.
Long-term skin regeneration and renewal are ensured by promoting the development of collagen and elastin, which leaves the skin feeling refreshed and revitalised.
WHAT ARE THE ADVANTAGES OF THE SMAS (HIFU) Lifting PROCEDURE?
Lifts and tightens deep facial tissues.
Reduces sagging skin.
Defines jawline and cheek contours.
Stimulates collagen production.
Non-surgical and minimally invasive.
Long-lasting results.
Minimal downtime.
Improves overall skin elasticity.
Natural-looking facial rejuvenation.
Treatment step-by-step
Form Submission & Consultation

The first step is filling out a small brief form with important information about your health, any contraindications and expectations. After this you have consultation with a specialist: the expert analyzes your form, identifies the areas requiring correction, and selects the optimal HIFU protocol to target the SMAS layer and achieve a noticeable lifting effect.
Before Treatment

It is recommended to avoid intense tanning for 2–4 weeks before the session, keep the skin well-hydrated, and temporarily stop using aggressive active skincare products according to your specialist’s advice. A full list of guidelines is available on the procedure page.
During Treatment

The specialist will cleanse the skin, mark the treatment areas and perform HIFU at the required depth to stimulate the SMAS layer and trigger collagen production. You may feel short bursts of warmth or mild discomfort — a normal response to focused ultrasound. The session lasts approximately 30–90 minutes depending on the treated area.
After Treatment

After the session, the specialist will soothe the skin if necessary, remove the markings and provide individual recommendations: avoiding intense heat treatments, direct sun exposure and maintaining proper hydration. Also detailed aftercare instructions you can find on the procedure page.

About the service

Are you looking for a way to improve skin texture, reduce pigmentation spots or treat acne? IPL Phototherapy (Intense Pulsed Light) is an innovative and effective technique created to address all these issues. Short, powerful light bursts focus on trouble spots, such as redness, pigmentation or damaged blood vessels to treat them. This procedure activates the skin's natural regeneration, so leaving it smoother, more even-toned and healthier. We are waiting for you at our London clinic.
Benefits IPL Phototherapy
Hyperpigmentation
Fine Lines & Wrinkles
Blemishes & Acne
Redness & Vascular Issues
Regeneration
Even Skin Tone
Anti-Dullness Effect
Skin Sensitivity
Stretch Mark Reduction
Anti-Ageing
How does the procedure work?

During IPL Phototherapy, a specialised device emits light pulses that are absorbed by melanin (in the case of pigmentation) or hemoglobin (for vascular issues). These pulses also promote skin rejuvenation by heating and destroying damaged cells. You'll notice lighter spots, less irritation, and better texture following the first session. A full course of treatments ensures long-lasting rejuvenating effects that can last up to a year.
IPL phototherapy makes the complexion more even by reducing freckles, dark spots and other pigmentation problems.
IPL stimulates the development of collagen and elastin, which lessens the appearance of fine lines and wrinkles.
The treatment targets acne-causing bacteria, reducing active breakouts and preventing future blemishes.
IPL balances the skin tone by successfully reducing visible veins, broken capillaries and redness.
By increasing cellular regeneration, this therapy encourages long-term rejuvenation and skin renewal.
IPL corrects uneven skin tone brought on by scarring, age spots or sun damage.
IPL fights dullness and leaves the skin renewed by focusing on the skin's deeper layers.
IPL is a safe and efficient procedure for a range of skin types and produces visible results with little to no pain.
The treatment stimulates collagen and improves skin texture, which lessens the appearance of stretch marks.
IPL phototherapy lessens drooping skin, wrinkles and age spots, among other obvious signs of aging.
WHAT ARE THE ADVANTAGES OF THE IPL Phototherapy PROCEDURE?
Reduces pigmentation and dark spots.
Minimizes redness and broken capillaries.
Improves skin tone and texture.
Stimulates collagen production.
Treats acne and inflammation.
Non-invasive and painless.
Quick procedure with minimal downtime.
Suitable for multiple skin areas.
Enhances overall skin clarity and glow.
Treatment step-by-step
Form Submission & Consultation

We begin with a short form where you indicate your health status, contraindications, skin phototype, expectations, and desired results. After this, you will have an individual consultation with a specialist who reviews the form and selects the optimal IPL settings for your skin.
Before Treatment

Avoid intense tanning for 2–4 weeks, discontinue photosensitizing products (as advised by the specialist), and maintain good skin hydration. The full list of pre-procedure instructions is available on the procedure page.
During Treatment

The specialist will cleanse your skin, apply a protective gel if needed, and choose appropriate device settings for your phototype. During the treatment you may feel mild warmth or quick “snapping” sensations — this is normal. A session usually takes 15–40 minutes depending on the area.
After Treatment

After treatment, the specialist will soothe your skin, remove the gel, and provide personalised aftercare guidelines: avoid sun exposure, apply SPF, moisturise the skin, and avoid heat-based treatments for 24–48 hours. The full list of recommendations is provided on the website.

About the service

Looking for a natural way to rejuvenate your skin, reduce scars or diminish stretch marks? PRP Therapy (Platelet-Rich Plasma Therapy) is a successful and effective treatment that restores skin health by utilising your body's natural resources. Throughout the procedure, platelet-rich plasma is drawn from your blood and injected into problem areas. This plasma enhances blood circulation, speeds up tissue repair, encourages the creation of collagen and elastin, and stimulates natural regeneration processes. The result is firmer, more luminous and regenerated skin without a long recovery period. We are waiting for you at our London clinic.
Benefits PRP Therapy
Skin Regeneration
Skin Elasticity
Reduction in Scars
Fine Lines & Wrinkles
Hyperpigmentation
Anti-Ageing
Hydration
Blemishes & Acne
How does the procedure work?

PRP Therapy involves several steps: The first step, we draw a small amount of your blood and process it in a centrifuge to separate the platelet-rich plasma. Then, using tiny needles, this plasma is injected into trouble spots, this action promoting skin renewal, triggering the creation of collagen and elastin, and also stimulating natural regeneration processes. Your skin looks healthier and more vibrant after the first treatment, and a full course guarantees results that last up to a year.
PRP promotes cellular regeneration and improves skin renewal by promoting the body's natural healing.
The therapy increases the suppleness, firmness and resilience of the skin by increasing the synthesis of collagen and elastin.
By promoting tissue regeneration, PRP significantly decreases the appearance of scars, including stretch marks, surgical scars, and acne scars.
The treatment reduces the visibility of fine lines and wrinkles through collagen regeneration and skin structure improvement.
PRP evens out skin tone and lessens hyperpigmentation for a more glowing appearance.
PRP fights the symptoms of aging by revitalizing the skin, restoring its vitality, and enhancing its general texture and tone.
This procedure increases skin hydration, by strengthening the skin's natural ability to retain moisture.
PRP helps reduce acne and blemishes by improving skin health and reducing inflammation.
WHAT ARE THE ADVANTAGES OF THE PRP Therapy PROCEDURE?
Stimulates natural collagen production.
Improves skin texture and tone.

Treatment step-by-step
Form Submission & Consultation

The first step is filling out a small brief form with important information about your health, any contraindications and expectations. After this you have a personal consultation, the specialist discusses your expectations, explains the procedure and determines the areas for PRP injections.
Before Treatment

It is recommended to avoid taking anticoagulants/aspirin beforehand (consult your doctor), avoid intense tanning and have a good meal and hydration before the visit. The full list of guidelines is available on the procedure page.
During Treatment

A small amount of your blood is collected and centrifuged to obtain platelet-rich plasma. The specialist cleanses the skin and injects PRP or applies it via microneedling to the chosen areas. You may feel slight pricking or discomfort. The session usually lasts 30–60 minutes.
After Treatment

Mild swelling or bruising may occur — the specialist will advise how to reduce it, maintain hygiene, and use cooling if needed. Avoid intense exercise, saunas and direct sun exposure for 24–72 hours. The complete aftercare list is also on the website.

About the service

Laser hair removal is a modern, effective and safe procedure that helps women eliminate unwanted hair, leaving their body smooth and well-groomed. Laser light that deeply penetrates the skin and permanently eliminates hair without causing harm to the surrounding tissues . Thanks to this method, you can forget about daily shaving or other temporary hair removal techniques. Laser Hair removal offers a quick and painless solution to obtain a perfect appearance.
Benefits Laser Hair Removal
Unwanted Hair
Skin Sensitivity
Tone & Texture
How does the procedure work?

To reduce discomfort and protect the epidermis, the specialist cleanses the skin and applies a cooling gel before the session. The laser beam is carefully aimed at the hair follicles, where it destroys the follicular structure and absorbs the color in the hair. This halts hair growth, leaving the skin smooth.
Laser hair reduction efficiently targets and eliminates unwanted hair, in addition to offering long-lasting smoothness and drastically lowering regrowth.
The procedure is gentle on the skin, minimizing irritation and suitable for sensitive areas like the bikini line, underarms, and face.
Laser hair removal leaves skin smooth and even, improving skin texture, by removing stubble and ingrown hairs.
WHAT ARE THE ADVANTAGES OF THE Laser Hair Removal?
Long-lasting hair reduction.
Smooths the skin.
Fast and effective sessions.
Reduces ingrown hairs.
Saves time compared to shaving/waxing.
Minimally painful procedure.
Works on multiple body areas.
Precise targeting of hair follicles.
Suitable for both men and women.
Treatment step-by-step
Consultation Form and Assessment

The first step is completing a short information form with information about your health, potential contraindications, prior treatment experience, and expectations is the first step. The next step is a personal consultation when our specialist determines the best laser settings and develops a customized treatment plan while analyzing your skin phototype, hair color and structure treatment area.
Before Treatment

It is advised to stay out of the sun, tanning beds, and self-tanning creams for two to four weeks before the treatment. Waxing, sugaring, or epilators should not be utilized; the treatment region must be shaved 24 hours before the session. Avoiding aggressive cosmetic procedures and products is also advised. The full list of preparation guidelines is available on the procedure page.
During Treatment

The specialist cleans the skin and applies laser treatment to the chosen area during the session. Without harming the surrounding tissues, the laser energy progressively destroys the pigment in the hair follicle. You might feel a little pinching, tingling, or warmth. Depending on the region being treated, the process usually takes 15 to 60 minutes.
After Treatment

After the procedure, there may be brief redness, slight swelling or increased skin sensitivity; these side effects often go away fast. But we advised to apply SPF protection, avoid direct sun exposure and overheating, and adhere to the specialist's post-treatment maintenance instructions.

title: Root canal treatment
copy: Root canal treatment
Root canal treatment eliminates discomfort and protects your natural teeth for years to come. Schedule an appointment in London online today.
How we can help you?

We at KClinics know how important it is to keep your natural teeth in order to preserve your oral health. So our specialists in endodontic therapies can help you avoid teeth extracted by successfully treating dental infections or damages. We guarantee you reliable and painless root canal therapy without discomfort and maintain your natural smile. We are waiting for you at our London clinic.
What Is Endodontics?

The area of dentistry known as endodontics is dedicated to identifying and treating concerns related to the tissues of the dental pulp and roots. Root canal therapy, which includes extracting inflamed or diseased pulp, cleaning and sanitising the root canal, and sealing it to stop additional damage, for all this treatment we can also say "endodontic treatment."
When do you need to treat the Root Canal?
You might require root canal therapy if you experience any of there problem:
Persistent tooth pain: Continuous pain, especially when chewing or exerting pressurу.
Temperature Sensitivity: Persistent sensitivity to either high or low temperatures, even after the trigger has been eliminated.
Swollen Gums: Recurrent pimples or swelling on the gums next to the tooth that is afflicted.
Severe cracks or deep decay: damage or decay tooth that reaches the pulp, leaving it vulnerable to infection.
Damage to the Tooth: Trauma that causes harm to the pulp and nerves of the teeth.
The Cost of Root Canal Treatment
At our clinic, the cost of a consultation is £50, which is taken as a deposit. This deposit can be fully used towards any future treatments you decide to proceed with.
How Is Root Canal Treatment Performed?
Several crucial steps are usually persist in root canal therapy

Examination: To determine the state of the tooth and the degree of pulp injury, a comprehensive check-up is conducted using digital X-rays. Anaesthesia: To make sure that the process for patients is painless, we use local anaesthetic Tooth Isolation: Also during the procedure, the tooth is kept dry and isolated from other teeth.

Access Opening: To gain access to the pulp chamber for root canals, the dentist takes a tiny incision in the tooth’s crown. Pulp Removal: The inflamed or infected pulp inside the tooth is removed using specialised tools.
Cleaning: Using rotary tools and antiseptic chemicals, the canals are completely cleared of germs and pulp residues. Shaping: The canals are shaped to prepare them for proper filling.

Sealing: After cleaning and preparing the treated area, the canals are filled with a biocompatible material (gutta-percha) that prevents reinfection. Temporary Filling: A temporary filling is used to save your tooth until a permanent restoration is done.
Crown or Filling: In order to fully restore functions and strength, a tooth that has treated root canal typically needs a permanent restoration, like a crown or filling.

Benefits of Choosing KClinics for Your Endodontic Care
Comfort-Focused Procedures: To ensure a painless experience, we use anaesthetic and work delicately during procedures.
Our instruments, which range from rotary instruments to X-ray, are made to provide accurate and effective oral care.
Every patient is given a customised treatment plan with an emphasis on their dental health and comfort.
Our endodontists are dedicated to maintaining your natural teeth and enhancing your oral health.
Our staff will provide you thorough aftercare advice after your root canal procedure to ensure a speedy recovery. What to expect and how to take care of your tooth after therapy
After the procedure, your mouth can feel numb for a few hours. To avoid unintentional biting, avoid eating until the feeling returns.

Over-the-counter painkillers such as ibuprofen or acetaminophen are typically effective in mild soreness or tenderness, which patients have for a few days. Observe your dentist’s advice when taking medicine.
Care Tips for a Smooth Recovery
Oral Hygiene : To maintain the region clean without causing any damage to the treated tooth, keep up your good oral hygiene routine, which includes gentle brushing and flossing.
Eating and Drinking: To reduce strain on the treated tooth, limit your intake to soft foods for the first days. Avoid eating crunchy or hard foods that can be aggravating for the treatment region.

Monitoring and Follow-Up : Swelling and Sensitivity: It's common to have some edema or mild sensitivity upon biting. But, get in touch with our office immediately if you feel excruciating pain, swelling that becomes worse or infection symptoms like fever or pus.

Follow-Up Appointment: It is essential that you show up for any follow-up visits that your dentist has set up. During these appointments,we may be placed for a permanent repair like a crown and make sure the treated tooth heals properly.

Long-Term Care: Permanent Restoration: The majority of teeth that have had root canal therapy will need a crown or filling, this helps fully regain their strength and function. To save your teeth, make sure to plan and make new appointments for this.
Frequent Dental Visits: Keeping an eye on the state of your treated tooth over time and preserving your oral cavity depend on routine dental check-ups.
[elementor-template id="9210"]
Frequently Asked Questions

A root canal is a dental procedure used to treat an infection or inflammation inside the root of a tooth. To stop additional infection, the process entails cleaning, sealing, and removing any dead or infected pulp from the root canals.
The process is usually painless because of contemporary anaesthetic techniques. Compared to the agony of a tooth infection, you can experience some discomfort both during and after the procedure.
Depending on the case complexity, a root canal treatment can take from 60 to 90 minutes and is usually completed in one or more visits.
A crown is typically advised after root canal therapy to restore the tooth’s function and stop it from decaying .
Root canal therapy may not be necessary if cavities are found and treated early. Preventing serious harm requires maintaining good oral hygiene and scheduling every 6 month dental check-ups.
A tooth that has received root canal therapy can endure for many years, and in certain situations, forever, with the right maintenance.
If the tooth has significant damage and the pulp is infected, the only alternative may be tooth extraction. Root canal treatment is the only way to save the tooth.
No, other teeth are unaffected by root canal therapy. Only the afflicted tooth is the target of this therapy.
Yes, even after treating a root canal, a tooth may still be vulnerable to cavities if it is not taken care of properly. This emphasises how crucial it is to apply a crown for future protection.
After treating a root canal, the tooth may occasionally have a small colour change. For a more aesthetic appearance, we advise taking crown placement or teeth whitening.

Yes, you can combine this procedure with other body sculpting techniques like RF Lifting or Endosphere, but before that, it is better to consult a specialist.
We can improve the results in an additional session. But if necessary, fillers can be dissolved by using a special solution that removes this material quickly and safely.

Yes, it is possible to treat several body parts in a single session.

The procedure is suitable for areas such as thighs, buttocks, abdomen, arms, back, and even calves.
Yes, vacuum massage successfully lessens the appearance of cellulite, by increasing skin tone, lymphatic drainage and blood circulation.

The procedure’s effects may persist for several months, depending on the client’s lifestyle. To maintain the outcomes, maintenance sessions are recommended once every one to two months.
Yes, roller massage can be combined with vacuum massage, body wraps, or skincare treatments. This helps achieve a comprehensive and enhanced effect.
Yes, the procedure is suitable for sensitive skin, because the intensity of the device’s impact is adjusted individually to avoid irritation.

Your skin gets firmer, more moisturized and smoother after the first treatment. A complete course of treatments significantly improves skin texture, lowers the appearance of cellulite and reduces body volume.

Yes, body wraps are suitable for all skin types. However, it is recommended to use calming or moisturizing wraps for sensitive skin.

title: IPL Rejuvenation
copy: IPL Rejuvenation
The Perfect for issues such as redness, acne and uneven skin tone. Schedule an appointment in London online today.
Duration: 20-30 minutes per session
Number of Sessions: 4-6 procedures with intervals of 3-4 weeks
Mild Pain
Instant Results: lasting up to 12 months
An effective solution for correcting vascular lesions, providing a natural, even skin tone.
Free
Pay per session or buy a bundle and save.

About the service

IPL (Intense Pulsed Light) is a modern, non-invasive procedure that uses broad-spectrum light to target pigmentation, capillaries and other skin imperfections. Deeply into the skin, the light enhances texture, encourages collagen synthesis, and promotes regeneration. We are waiting for you at our London clinic.
Benefits IPL Rejuvenation
Hydration
Dullness & Dryness
Hyperpigmentation
Acne & Blemishes
Skin Sensitivity
Tone & Texture
How does the procedure work?

During an IPL phototherapy session intense pulsed light enters the skin and targets haemoglobin or melanin in blood vessels. Without causing harm to the surrounding tissues, the light energy is transformed into heat that can coagulate dilated capillaries, break down pigmentation spots, or promote the formation of collagen. After the first session, the skin's texture greatly improves and it becomes smoother and more bright.
IPL phototherapy improves the skin's natural hydration process by encouraging cell renewal and collagen synthesis, as a result skin is deeply hydrated and renewed.
This procedure improves the texture of the skin by promoting natural cell turnover and increasing circulation. This lessens dry or lifeless areas and makes the skin healthier and more radiant.
IPL breaks down dark spots brought on by ageing or sun damage by targeting areas of uneven pigmentation. As a result skin has a brightening tone.
IPL therapy targets bacteria in the pores to effectively reduce inflammation brought on by acne. It minimises breakouts and stops new blemishes by washing off dirt, oil and pollutants.
This procedure can be customised for those with sensitive skin, guaranteeing a non-irritating, mild yet efficient method of enhancing skin tone and texture.
IPL leaves the skin firm, glowing, and uniformly toned by eliminating impurities, reducing fine wrinkles, and smoothing out uneven skin texture.
WHAT ARE THE ADVANTAGES OF THE IPL Rejuvenation PROCEDURE?
Improves overall skin tone.
Reduces pigmentation and sunspots.
Minimizes redness and broken capillaries.
Stimulates collagen production.
Smooths skin texture.
Reduces fine lines.
Helps treat acne and acne marks.
Quick, non-invasive procedure.
Little to no downtime.
Areas Treated with IPL Rejuvenation
The appearance of different parts of the face and body can be enhanced and restored using this adaptable process. The following are the most often used therapy areas:

Forehead: Improves skin texture and minimises fine lines and pigmentation. Around the eyes: Reduces fine wrinkles, crow’s feet and dark circles. Cheeks: Improves tone, reduces redness, and diminishes acne scars. Chin: tightens the skin and lessens redness or discolouration.
Reduces uneven tone and smoothes skin texture. Reduces pigmentation and wrinkles brought on by ageing or sun exposure.
Effectively enhances the texture and tone of the skin on the thighs, shoulders, back, or abdomen. Often used to address pigmentation or imperfections after weight loss or pregnancy.
Treatment step-by-step
Form Submission & Consultation

The first step is filling out a small brief form with important information about your health, any contraindications, expectations and desired outcomes. After this, every client has a personal consultation with our specialist. During the consultation, the specialist analyse your phototype, reviews your form, and helps determine the optimal treatment areas. This allows us to create a personalized and effective IPL rejuvenation plan tailored to your needs.
Before Treatment

For optimal IPL results, it is essential to avoid tanning for at least 2–4 weeks before the procedure and maintain regular skin hydration. It is also recommended to stop using photosensitizing skincare products unless your specialist advises differently. The complete Before Care guideline is available on our website.
During Treatment

The specialist will cleanse the treatment area, apply a protective gel, and select the appropriate IPL settings according to your phototype. You may feel mild flashes of warmth or a quick “snapping” sensation similar to a rubber band—this is completely normal. The session typically lasts 20–40 minutes depending on the number of areas treated.
After Treatment

After the session, the specialist removes the gel, soothes the skin, and gives you also personalized After Care instructions: avoiding sun exposure, using SPF, keeping the skin hydrated, and avoiding heat treatments for 24–48 hours. All After Care suggestions are also available on the treatment page on our website.

TOP 3 COMPLEMENTARY TREATMENTS
Carbon Laser Peel
Chemical Peels
Antioxidant Therapy
Frequently Asked Questions
No, the process is comfortable and completely painless. But some clients can feel a little warm or tingly feeling during the therapy.
It is advised to have 4-6 sessions spaced out by 3–4 weeks. Your skin type, condition and desired outcomes will determine the amount of sessions.
After the first session your skin becomes more even, smoother and brighter. Following the completion of the entire course of therapy, the final outcomes become apparent.
Depending on your lifestyle, skin type and post-treatment skin care, results may last up to a year. Regular IPL sessions can help prolong the outcomes.

IPL is most effective on lighter skin types. Because darker skin tones are more likely to experience burns or hyperpigmentation, so in this situation our specialist could suggest alternate treatments like chemical peels or microneedling.
Yes, IPL lessens acne-related inflammation and enhances skin texture by reducing scars and post-acne markings.
Yes, the process evens out the skin tone and successfully minimises pigmentation, freckles, and sunspots.
The face, neck, décolleté, hands and even the body (back, shoulders, and belly) can all be treated with IPL. The process is adaptable and appropriate for a variety of areas.

To improve outcomes, IPL can be used in conjunction with other procedures including chemical peels, face masks, or microneedling. Nonetheless, it is important to observe the recommended intervals between treatments.
