5.3. You commit to heeding any advice given by the experts at KClinics and to immediately inform us of any changes in your medical condition that may affect the procedures.
5.4 . Clients are required to inform KClinics immediately of any changes in their health condition, because that could affect the safety or efficacy of the procedures.
6.1. Payment for services is required in full at the time of booking, including all applicable taxes and fees.
6.2. You must give us at least 48 hours' notice before the appointment if you need to cancel or reschedule your reservation.
6.3. If you cancel on time you can get a refund in accordance with our refund policy.
7.1. Our services are available only to clients from the age of 18 and over.
7.2. In case of doubt, we may request valid personal identification to confirm the client's age.
8.1. The information provided on our website is not a substitute for professional medical advice.
8.2. We advise speaking with your doctor before beginning any procedure.

8.3 . KClinics takes all necessary precautions to ensure the safety of its procedures. However, individual results may vary, and clients acknowledge the potential risks inherent to aesthetic treatments.
9.1. All financial transactions are processed through certified payment gateways compliant with PCI DSS standards to ensure the security of your data.
10.1 . Right to Compensation
If the service does not live up to the claimed level, you have the following rights under the Consumer Rights Act of 2015:
Request a free repeat of the service, if possible.
Request a partial or full refund if corrections cannot be made.

10.2 If you think the service was provided incorrectly, you can submit a complaint by email support@kclinics.co.uk with a thorough explanation of the problem. We will contact you during 7 business days to resolve the matter.
We actively cooperate with financial institutions and law enforcement in all cases of suspected fraudulent activity.
12.1. We may update these terms from time to time by amending this page. You should check this page periodically to ensure you agree with any changes.
12.2. Notification of Changes
We will notify users of significant changes to these terms via email or a prominent notice on our website. Continuing to use the website after changes implies acceptance of the updated terms.
If you have any questions, you can contact us via:
Email: support@kclinics.co.uk

title: Website Privacy & Terms
copy: Website Privacy & Terms
This Privacy Policy describes your rights, who may access your personal information,the legal basis for such use, who has access to your personal data, and your rights. Please read it carefully.
We are KCLINICS GROUP LIMITED, a company registered under number 14985949.
Registered address: 4 Charterhouse Bldgs, Goswell Rd., London EC1M 7AN

We handle your personal information in compliance with our Privacy Policy because we value your privacy. Since we are the "data controller" of your personal information, meaning we determine how and why your data is processed.

You have the right to object if your personal information is processed for direct marketing (including any associated profiling) or for our legitimate business reasons. For more information about your rights and how to exercise them, please see the "Your Rights" section.
Personal data refers to any information that identifies you personally, either directly (e.g., your name) or indirectly (e.g., information about your use of our products and services).
We could get the following information about you:
Contact information: Your name, phone number, and email address to communicate with you in response to your inquiry via our website or regarding products and services we provide to you.
Correspondence: Any extra personal information you give us via phone, email, mail, or other channels, such as leaving a comment on our website.
Responses to surveys: Information from surveys we use for research purposes, should you choose to participate.
Transaction details: Information about transactions you make through our website to fulfill your orders.
Website usage data: Information about your visits, such as traffic data, location data, web logs, and other communication data needed for analytics or other uses, as well as the resources you use.
What are cookies?

Through cookies, we gather data about how you use our website. Cookies are little files that are saved on your computer, tablet, or smartphone that let websites remember your identity and visit data. Cookies assist in displaying information on our website tailored to your interests. Most major websites use cookies.
What types of cookies are used on this website?
Cookies used by us and our business partners on our website can be categorized as follows:

Essential cookies: In order to give you the services you have requested, certain cookies are necessary. For example, a cookie that guarantees communication between your browser and our website or allows you to access your account on our website. Without these cookies, you cannot use our website.

Cookies for analysis: We employ analytics cookies to get insight into how people engage with our website. For example, instead of counting the overall number of times a site or feature is used, we measure the number of distinct users that visit our site or utilize certain features. This helps us improve the website's functionality.

You can control cookies through settings in the majority of browsers. However, restricting cookies may negatively impact your user experience, as it will no longer be personalized for you. It may also prevent saving settings such as login details.
How We Use Your Personal Data
Provision of services: To complete your orders, answer your questions and give you access to our goods and services, including scheduling and associated services.
Communication: To answer your questions, notify you of service modifications, confirm your bookings, and send critical information related to your services.
Personalization: To adjust the material that appears on the website according to your preferences and past interactions with us.
Marketing: We may send you information about sales, exclusive deals or new services with your permission.
Improving service quality: We will employ analytics data, survey data, and feedback analysis to improve our products and services.
Compliance with laws: To carry out our duties under the law and regulations, including tax reporting and accounting.
We process your data based on the following legal grounds
Consent: When you give your express permission for processing (for example, marketing).
Contractual obligation: In cases when processing is required to carry out a mutually agreed upon arrangement (e.g., scheduling procedures).
Legitimate interests: When processing is necessary for our commercial objectives, such monitoring user activity or guaranteeing website security.
Legal obligations: When we are required to store or process data by law.
Your data may be shared with the following:
Service providers: Companies assisting us in fulfilling orders, processing payments, conducting marketing campaigns, or providing technical support.
Platforms for analytics: Services that aid in the analysis of website user activity.
Legal authorities: When mandated by law, such as for legal compliance or inquiries.
International Data Transfers: We utilize standard contractual clauses or other legally permissible measures to secure your data if it is moved outside of the European Economic Area (EEA).
We only keep your personal information for as long as is required to achieve the goals specified in this policy, including:
Data related to your orders is retained for the duration of service provision and any legally required period thereafter.
Contact information for marketing communications is retained until you withdraw your consent.
We anonymize or remove data when it is no longer required.
You have the following rights under GDPR:
Right of Access: Request confirmation of whether we process your data and access to that data.
Right to Rectification : Ask that incomplete or erroneous data be corrected.
Right to Erasure : Request deletion of your data if there are no legal grounds for retention.
Right to Restrict Processing: Put a temporary stop to how your data is processed.
Right to Data Portability : Get a structured copy of your data.
Right to Object : You have the right to object to how your data is used for marketing or other uses.
Right to Withdraw Consent: You have the freedom to change your mind about data processing at any moment.
To exercise your rights, contact support@kclinics.com.
KCLINICS GROUP LIMITED makes every effort to ensure the uninterrupted operation of the website and quality services. However:
We can't promise that there will never be any errors or technical problems with the website or its services.
We are not responsible for any indirect, incidental, or other types of damages (including loss of profit, data, or reputation) resulting from the use of our website or services.
Any content or materials supplied by third parties via our website are not our responsibility.
All content on the website, including text, pictures, graphics, and other materials, is either owned by KCLINICS GROUP LIMITED or used in accordance with legal agreements.
It is forbidden to use, share, or copy any content from our website without written permission.
Users who download or share any content without authorization may be held liable under applicable law.
We may allow users to leave reviews, comments, or other content ("User-Generated Content") on our website under the following conditions:
Rules compliance: User-generated content must not be illegal, offensive, or include any unsuitable content.
Moderation rights: Any user content that breaks our rules or is unrelated to the goal of the website may be reviewed, changed, or removed at our discretion.
Disclaimer: We are not responsible for the accuracy, completeness, or truthfulness of User-Generated Content posted by third parties.

All content on the website, including software, photos, text, branding, logos, and other assets, is either owned by KCLINICS GROUP LIMITED or used under license, and is protected by copyright or other intellectual property laws.
It is expressly forbidden to use, distribute, reproduce, or copy these contents in any way without our permission.
Violations of our rights may result in legal action.
Any disputes arising in connection with the use of this website or our services will be resolved under the laws of England and Wales.
Procedure for Resolving Disputes:
KCLINICS GROUP LIMITED must receive any claims and questions in writing at support@kclinics.com.
If the dispute cannot be resolved through negotiation, both parties agree to arbitration or court proceedings at the company’s registered location.
We reserve the right to escalate disputes to appropriate regulatory or legal authorities if necessary.
We reserve the right to amend this Privacy Policy. The latest version will always be available on our website. We recommend periodically reviewing this page for updates.
If you have any questions about this Policy or wish to exercise your rights, please contact us:
Email: support@kclinics.com
Address: 4 Charterhouse Bldgs, Goswell Rd., London EC1M 7AN

title: Call Recording Privacy
copy: Call Recording Privacy

Because KClinics Group Limited respects your confidence, it is dedicated to maintaining openness about how processed personal information, including call recording. This policy describes the reasons for call recording, how your data is stored and used, your rights regarding these recordings, and how to exercise your rights.
We guarantee that all calls are recorded and processed in compliance with the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) and applicable UK legislation.
The purpose of call recording is to provide a record of incoming and outgoing calls which can:
Identify practice staff training needs
Protect practice staff from nuisance or abusive calls
Establish facts relating to incoming/outgoing calls made (e.g. disputes)
Identify any issues in practice processes with a view to improving them
Support clinicians with a record of telephone consultations in the event of complaint / medico-legal dispute.
The processing of your personal data through call recording is based on the following grounds:
Legitimate interests: We want to guarantee personnel training, service quality, and conflict resolution.
Performance of a contract: The necessity to process data to provide you with services in accordance with agreements.
Compliance with legal obligations: Adhering to legal requirements for data storage and processing.

We keep call recordings for as long as is required to achieve the purposes outlined above but no longer than permitted by law. The confidentiality and integrity of recordings are guaranteed by their storage in restricted-access, secure systems.
We do not share call recordings with third parties unless required by law or necessary to protect our legitimate interests (e.g., in the case of legal proceedings).
Under applicable data protection legislation, you have the following rights:
Right of access : Find out if your personal information is being processed and have access to it.
Right to rectificatio n: Request corrections to inaccurate or incomplete data.
Right to erasure : In some situations, you can ask for the data to be deleted.
Right to limit processing : Request the limitation of data processing under certain conditions.
Right to data portability : Receive your data in a structured, commonly used format and transfer it to another controller.
Right to object : You have the right to protest how your data is being used for legitimate purposes.
Right to withdraw consent: Withdraw your consent for data processing at any time, where processing is based on consent.
We ensure that all clients are informed about the call recording policy through:
Every call starts with an announcement.
webpage notifications.
informational resources in the clinic.
Integration of this information into contractual documentation.
KClinics Group Limited undertakes the following commitments:
Ensuring the confidentiality of all recorded data.
removing recordings after the allotted time, unless there are valid reasons to keep them longer.
granting prompt access to recordings to clients upon request.
quickly notifying customers of any modifications to the call recording policy.
If you have concerns or complaints about call recording, you can:
Contact our Data Protection Officer via email at support@kclinics.co.uk.
Submit a complaint to the Information Commissioner’s Office (ICO) if the issue is not resolved.
If you have questions or wish to exercise your rights, please contact us:
Email: support@kclinics.co.uk
Address: 4 Charterhouse Bldgs, Goswell Rd., London EC1M 7AN

title: CCTV Policy
copy: CCTV Policy

KClinics Group Limited is committed to ensuring the safety of our employees, clients and visitors, as well as managing its operations effectively. This policy describes our usage of CCTV systems, its purpose, data security, and the steps we take to guarantee adherence to applicable data protection regulations.
We guarantee that all CCTV data is processed in accordance with the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) and applicable UK legislation.
We use CCTV systems to achieve the following objectives:
Ensuring safety: Protecting employees, clients, and visitors.
Preventing and detecting crimes: In this including theft, vandalism or other unlawful activities.
Providing evidence: Supplying objective evidence to resolve any disputes or complaints.
Supporting investigations: Assisting law enforcement agencies in investigating incidents.
Access control: Monitoring access to company premises.
CCTV systems cover open areas within our premises to ensure transparency:
Cameras are installed in waiting areas, reception areas, corridors and other publicly accessible spaces.
Important: Private spaces such as restrooms and treatment rooms are not equipped with cameras.
All cameras are openly placed, and their presence is indicated with appropriate signage to inform clients and staff.
The processing of CCTV data is based on:
Legitimate interests: Ensuring safety, preventing crimes, and resolving disputes.
Legal obligations: Adherence to the laws governing incident investigation and data protection.
We adhere the high standards for CCTV data protection:
Retention period: Unless an extension request is submitted, recordings are automatically erased after 30 days.
Secure systems: Recordings are stored in encrypted systems with multi-level password protection, and all access is logged.
Restrictions on access: CCTV recordings are only accessible to authorized persons.
CCTV recordings may be accessed by:
Personnel with authorization: Only for the reasons specified in this policy.
Law enforcement organizations: upon request for incident inquiry.
Other organizations: If required to satisfy the business's justifiable objectives (e.g., insurance firms).
The transfer of data is subject to thorough review to ensure compliance with GDPR and to verify the validity of the request.
To submit a request for CCTV data:
Send a written request via email to support@kclinics.co.uk.
Indicate when, where and when you plan to visit the CCTV-monitored property.
After reviewing your request, we will get back to you in 30 days.
CCTV systems are regularly checked to make sure data protection regulations are being followed.
KClinics Group Limited takes full responsibility for the proper use of CCTV recordings and enforces disciplinary measures in the event of policy breaches.
Regular reviews and updates are made to this policy. 28.01.2015 was the last update.
Any major changes will be communicated to clients via our website or informational materials at our clinics.
If you have any questions regarding CCTV usage or data protection, please contact us:
Email: support@kclinics.co.uk
Address: 4 Charterhouse Bldgs, Goswell Rd., London EC1M 7AN
KClinics Group Limited is committed to transparency and protecting your personal data and rights.

title: Cancellations/Refunds
copy: Cancellations/Refunds

Thank you for choosing KClinics for your treatments. This policy outlines the terms and conditions regarding cancellations, refunds, and transfers of treatments, products, gift cards, and pre-paid packages. Our goals are to uphold a high level of service, guarantee transparency, and safeguard your rights.
This policy complies with the Consumer Rights Act, General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR), and other applicable UK legislation.

Refunds are not available for treatments, products, gift cards, pre-paid packages, or treatments that were scheduled and paid for online. Exceptions are outlined below for specific terms related to online purchases.

Please get in touch with the clinic management at the place where the purchase or reservation was made if you have an unpleasant response after using a treatment or product. The manager will provide consultation and suggest alternative treatments or products as appropriate.
This policy does not affect your statutory rights.
Refunds for Prepaid Gift Cards & Packages Purchased Online

We look forward to having you visit our clinic and hope you are happy with your purchase. However, for online purchases of pre-paid packages or gift cards, you may request a full refund within 14 days of purchase.
Conditions for refunds:
Refund requests must be sent by email together with the date of purchase and proof of payment.
Refunds are not available for used or partially used pre-paid packages or gift cards.
After the 14-day cooling-off period, the standard refund policy applies, and refunds are no longer available.
Please be aware that purchases made in the clinic are exempt from the 14-day cooling-off period.
We are aware that things might change. Therefore we respectfully ask for 48 hours' notice of cancellation in order to maintain a high standard of service for all customers.
Terms for cancellations:
If the customer cancels less than 48 hours prior to the planned appointment or does not show up, we have the right to keep the deposit or treatment cost.
For pre-paid packages, if the customer does not show up or cancels within 48 hours, the entire cost of the treatment will be lost.
Please speak with the clinic manager about your alternatives if you become ill within 48 hours of your appointment.
Pre-paid packages: Valid for 12 months from the purchase date.
Gift cards: Valid for 12 months from the purchase date.

Please be careful to arrange appointments prior to the expiration date in order to prevent losing your treatments. Once the validity period expires, pre-paid packages and gift cards become non-refundable.
If you are unable to complete treatments due to illness, contact the clinic manager for further assistance.
Gift cards, pre-paid packages, services and treatments that have been purchased cannot be transferred to another person.
Pre-paid packages for specific treatment areas cannot be transferred to other areas.
For online purchases, this policy is subject to the 14-day cooling-off period.
Adverse Reactions : The clinic manager will help address your issue by providing consultations or offering other procedures if you have an adverse response after a treatment or product usage.

Force Majeure : In cases of force majeure (e.g., natural disasters, government restrictions), appointments may be rescheduled without any refunds. Contact the clinic manager to discuss possible options.

Medical Conditions : Please get in touch with the clinic management, if you are unable to finish your pre-paid package due to a chronic medical condition. Supporting documentation may be required for adjustments.
This policy does not cancel your statutory rights under UK consumer protection laws. So if you think your rights have been damaged you can get more help from the appropriate regulatory agency.
If you have any concerns regarding this policy or its application:
Send an email to support@kclinics.co.uk with the specifics of your issue.
A clinic manager will analyze your complaint and work to find a quick solution.
We reserve the right to update this policy as needed. Information about updates will be posted on our website or in our clinics. The last update was made on 28.01.2024.
For inquiries or assistance, please contact us:
Email: support@kclinics.co.uk
Address: 4 Charterhouse Bldgs, Goswell Rd., London EC1M 7AN

title: Complaints Procedure
copy: Complaints Procedure

Thank you for choosing KClinics for your treatments and trusting us with your care. This policy describes our complaints process, which is intended to guarantee that any issues you may have are resolved in a timely, professional and transparent manner. Our objective is to deliver a transparent and equitable complaints procedure that puts your rights and pleasure on the first place while upholding the highest service standards.
This policy complies with the Consumer Rights Act, General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR), and other applicable UK legislation.
You can submit a complaint in writing. For efficiency, we recommend written complaints via:
Email: support@kclinics.co.uk
Post: Send your complaint to the clinic’s address.
2.1 What to Include in Your Complaint:
If your complaint is related to service or is a clinical complaint or ‘other’
Who or what has caused your concerns. If you are complaining about a member of staff, give their name and position (if you know it).
Where and when the events took place.
What action you have already taken, if any.
What outcome you want from your complaint.
Please include any supporting evidence such as photographs or other documents.
Within three business days.
4.1 Preliminary Review
After reviewing your complaint, the complaints manager will decide if it qualifies for additional investigation.
If it does not meet the criteria, you will be notified in writing within 7 working days, including an explanation for the decision.
4.2 Investigation
If the complaint meets the criteria:
It will be assigned to an independent manager who has no involvement in your case.
For clinical complaints, the medical director or external experts (e.g., equipment manufacturers) may be consulted.
The investigation will include:
Analysis of provided evidence.
Statements from all parties involved.
Compliance is checked against the criteria of the clinic.
After receiving a complaint, we try to address it within 8 weeks. If additional time is required, you will be informed with an explanation and a revised timeline.
Upon agreement with you, complaints that can be handled in three working days will be closed. Written confirmation will be sent to you in the form of a "Resolution Summary."
In accordance with the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) and other relevant regulations, all complaints are addressed in confidence.
If you are disagree with the final response, you may file an appeal within 10 working days by: inna.k@kclinics.co.uk
Writing a letter outlining the aspects of the decision you disagree with.
Providing any new evidence to support your appeal.
The appeal will be reviewed by an independent expert who was not involved in the initial complaint process.
You can bring the issue up with outside groups if you're still dicagree after the appeal:
Citizens Advice: www.citizensadvice.org.uk.
Financial Ombudsman Service (for financial complaints): You must refer your case within 6 months of receiving the final decision.

To maintain efficiency and impartiality, we continuously assess and improve our complaints processing procedure. After your complaint is resolved, you may be invited to participate in a feedback survey to help us improve our services.

title: Health and Safety
copy: Health and Safety

Our property at KClinics is the health and safety of our clients, so we do our best to create a safe, clean and comfortable environment that also complies with all UK regulations and goes beyond them to provide the highest standards of protection and care.
UK Legislation
The Health and Safety at Work Act of 1974 and other laws require medical and cosmetic clinics to provide safe and healthy working environments for both its employees and patients.
We adhere to the requirements set by:
The National Health Service (NHS),
The UK Health Security Agency,
The Health and Safety Executive (HSE).
These specifications address all aspects of operations, from facility cleanliness to employee training. We regularly upgrade our equipment to meet or exceed established standards.
How We Ensure Safety
We maintain strict sterilization standards in all areas of our clinic:
After each usage, a multi-step disinfection procedure is applied to all surfaces, tools and equipment.
We use advanced disinfection products and technologies effective against bacteria, viruses and other pathogens.
Air conditioners with HEPA filters are installed in each treatment room to clean the air and get rid of germs, viruses and allergies.
Regular Inspections: Our facilities are subject to internal and external audits to ensure compliance with standards.
Condition Monitoring: In order to provide for our clients the best possible clinic conditions, we constantly check the temperature, humidity and air quality.
All our team undergo regular training and certification to comply with the latest safety standards.
We provide training seminars on medical equipment use, infection control and first aid.
Our staff is well-trained in emergency response procedures and strictly adheres to hygiene protocols.
Every instrument and piece of equipment are regularly serviced and comply with European safety standards.
We work only with trusted suppliers to ensure safe and effective treatments.
Why It Matters to Us

We are aware that your confidence in our clinic rests on our ability to keep you secure. Strict attention to safety and sterilization regulations greatly reduces the risk of infections or problems after treatments. At KClinics, your health is always our top priority, and we continuously implement the latest safety practices.
Additional Safety Measures
After every customer, the disposable gloves, masks, and gowns worn by all employees are changed.
Strict handwashing and disinfection protocols are followed.
We provide a hygienic and secure setting so you may relax and feel safe.
Every client is given a customized treatment plan tailored to their individual requirements.
To avoid sanitary or environmental hazards, we closely adhere to WHO guidelines for the disposal of medical waste.
Before each procedure, we do risk assessments, taking into account any possible allergies, contraindications or other client-specific conditions.
After therapy, we also stay in touch with our customers to check on their health and make sure that everythings is good.
The clinic has evacuation plans in place for emergencies such as fires or other hazards.
If required, our personnel are qualified and prepared to offer immediate medical aid.

title: Accessibility
copy: Accessibility

At KClinics, we strive to create an environment where every client, regardless of physical abilities, feels comfortable, safe, and confident. Understanding that every person has different needs, we take great care to make sure our services and facilities are available to everyone. Our goal is to provide high-quality care to each client and promote an inclusive atmosphere that meets the highest standards.
Our clinics are designed with the needs of clients with disabilities in mind:
Step-Free Access: Clients using wheelchairs or other mobility aids may visit our clinics with ease thanks to ramps and accessible entrances.
Wide Corridors: Clients in wheelchairs may easily move around the clinic because of the wide corridors.
We have taken care to provide specially designed restrooms for the comfort of clients with disabilities:
Adapted Facilities: Our restrooms are equipped with grab rails and emergency call buttons, to guarantee comfort and safety.
Professional Support from Staff: Our employees are always available to help customers with unique needs:
Assistance with Entrance: Our staff is on hand to assist you with entering the clinic or navigating inside the clinic .
Guidance to Treatment Rooms: To make sure you feel secure and supported, we offer easy access to all treatment rooms.
Personalised Care: Every step of your visit will be as comfortable as possible since our team is trained to modify all services to fit your particular needs.
We have created our waiting areas to accommodate the needs of all clients:
Dedicated Spaces for Mobility-Impaired Clients: Our waiting areas include specially designated spots for individuals who use wheelchairs or mobility aids.
Comfort and Space: All waiting areas are equipped with comfortable seating and enough space, to guarantee that customers using mobility aids can move about easily.
We are aware that every customer has different needs, and we promise to provide:
Adapted Treatments: We customize our processes and offerings to meet the needs of our customers who have sensory or physical disabilities.
Special Consultations: We provide consultations before any procedures, to address your unique needs and guarantee your utmost comfort during your stay.

To provide the best possible service, we encourage clients to inform us of their specific needs in advance. When making an online, phone, or email appointment, you can include this information. This aids us in making the preparations required to satisfy your needs.
For clients with sensory needs or visual impairments, we provide:
Clear Signage: Procedure menus, navigational signs, and informational boards are designed to be easily understood by all clients.
Accessible Communication: Our staff is available to help by written justifications or other means that best meet your needs.
To make our clinics as accessible as possible to all of our customers, we are always making improvements:
Parking for Clients with Disabilities: Parking spots are available as close to the entrance as possible.
Comfortable Waiting Areas: We provide cozy and spacious waiting areas for clients using mobility aids.

In our opinion at KClinics everyone should have simple access to high-quality healthcare. We are committed to enhancing our offerings in order to give each customer an outstanding experience that makes them feel appreciated, respected and at ease.
If you have any questions or suggestions about accessibility, please don’t hesitate to contact us. We are always happy to make your visit as comfortable as possible.

title: HydraGlowFacial Platinum Anti-Acne
copy: HydraGlowFacial Platinum Anti-Acne
Perfect for clearing breakouts, reducing inflammation, and achieving smooth, acne-free skin.
Duration: 60 minutes
Number of Sessions: 1-6 sessions with intervals of 1-2 weeks
Sensation Completely comfortable
Visible Results Immediately after the first session
Achieve clear, smooth and refreshed skin with our HydraGlowFacial Platinum Anti-Acne treatment, combining deep cleansing, targeted exfoliation, and advanced anti-acne serums!
Book a Consultation
Free
Buy a Package
from £[wbcr_php_snippet id="42508" usluga="133"]

About the service

This version of HydraGlowFacial Platinum is specifically designed to combat acne problems. Blackheads and inflammation-causing bacteria are removed from pores and the skin is completely cleaned of pollutants and extra sebum. The therapy calms the skin, reduces redness, and speeds up healing by using serums that include antioxidants and anti-inflammatory components. In addition to cleansing the skin, anti-acne products also enhance its texture, leaving it feeling renewed and healthy.
Benefits HydraGlowFacial Platinum Anti-Acne
Hydration
Texture & Tone
Blemishes & Acne
Hyperpigmentation
Wrinkles & Fine Lines
Sensitivity to Skin
Detoxification
Anti-Inflammatory Effect
Oil Control
HydraGlowFacial restores moisture and natural balance by intensively hydrating the skin with hyaluronic acid, peptides and antioxidants.
The process leaves the skin smooth, glowing and renewed by improving its tone and texture.
A HydraGlowFacial helps to lessen acne and stop new breakouts by cleaning pores and eliminating pollutants.
This procedure makes your face lighter and more even by reducing dark spots and balancing out skin tone.
HydraGlowFacial lessens the visibility of wrinkles and fine lines due to increasing collagen and skin flexibility.
This mild treatment provides effective exfoliation and hydration without irritation that is appropriate for sensitive skin.
Toxins are eliminated from the skin and its healthy look is restored with deep washing.
This procedure soothes sensitive skin by lowering redness and irritation.
HydraGlowFacial Anti-Acne reduces oiliness and the chance of fresh breakouts by controlling sebum production.
WHAT ARE THE ADVANTAGES OF THE HydraGlowFacial Platinum Anti-Acne PROCEDURE?
Deeply cleanses pores.
Removes excess oil and impurities.
Reduces acne breakouts.
Minimizes inflammation and redness.
Exfoliates dead skin cells.
Improves skin texture.
Hydrates and nourishes the skin.
Prevents future acne formation.
Safe and non-invasive treatment.
Treatment step-by-step
Cleansing & Exfoliation

In the first step, the skin undergoes gentle cleansing and exfoliation, where impurities and dead skin cells are softly removed. This smooths the texture of your skin and prepares it for the next active stages. Proper skin cleansing increases the effectiveness of the specialized Platinum serums.
Deep Pore Cleansing

Using HydraGlowFacial vacuum technology excess sebum, impurities, blackheads, and oxidized residues are removed from the pores. The process is comfortable and non-irritating. As a result, the skin becomes clean and the pores are optimally prepared for targeted Platinum therapy.
Anti-Acne Infusion

title: Refund and Returns Policy
copy: This is a sample page.
Overview
Our refund and returns policy lasts 30 days. If 30 days have passed since your purchase, we can’t offer you a full refund or exchange.
To be eligible for a return, your item must be unused and in the same condition that you received it. It must also be in the original packaging.

Several types of goods are exempt from being returned. Perishable goods such as food, flowers, newspapers or magazines cannot be returned. We also do not accept products that are intimate or sanitary goods, hazardous materials, or flammable liquids or gases.
Additional non-returnable items:
Gift cards
Downloadable software products
Some health and personal care items
To complete your return, we require a receipt or proof of purchase.
Please do not send your purchase back to the manufacturer.
There are certain situations where only partial refunds are granted:
Book with obvious signs of use
CD, DVD, VHS tape, software, video game, cassette tape, or vinyl record that has been opened.
Any item not in its original condition, is damaged or missing parts for reasons not due to our error.
Any item that is returned more than 30 days after delivery
Refunds
Once your return is received and inspected, we will send you an email to notify you that we have received your returned item. We will also notify you of the approval or rejection of your refund.
If you are approved, then your refund will be processed, and a credit will automatically be applied to your credit card or original method of payment, within a certain amount of days.
Late or missing refunds
If you haven’t received a refund yet, first check your bank account again.
Then contact your credit card company, it may take some time before your refund is officially posted.
Next contact your bank. There is often some processing time before a refund is posted.
If you’ve done all of this and you still have not received your refund yet, please contact us at {email address}.
Sale items
Only regular priced items may be refunded. Sale items cannot be refunded.
Exchanges
We only replace items if they are defective or damaged. If you need to exchange it for the same item, send us an email at {email address} and send your item to: {physical address}.
Gifts

If the item was marked as a gift when purchased and shipped directly to you, you’ll receive a gift credit for the value of your return. Once the returned item is received, a gift certificate will be mailed to you.

If the item wasn’t marked as a gift when purchased, or the gift giver had the order shipped to themselves to give to you later, we will send a refund to the gift giver and they will find out about your return.
Shipping returns
To return your product, you should mail your product to: {physical address}.

You will be responsible for paying for your own shipping costs for returning your item. Shipping costs are non-refundable. If you receive a refund, the cost of return shipping will be deducted from your refund.
Depending on where you live, the time it may take for your exchanged product to reach you may vary.
If you are returning more expensive items, you may consider using a trackable shipping service or purchasing shipping insurance. We don’t guarantee that we will receive your returned item.
Need help?
Contact us at {email} for questions related to refunds and returns.

title: Aesthetic Dentistry
copy: Aesthetic Dentistry

A smile is more than just a facial expression — it’s a key element of your style, confidence and overall attractiveness. Correcting dental flaws, improving functionality and creating a harmonious appearance are all possible with aesthetic dentistry. From teeth whitening to extensive restorations like veneers and bonding, our clinic offers a variety of therapies to improve your smile. Using innovative technology and a customised approach we are committed to creating a smile that surpasses your expectations and increases your confidence in every moment of life.
[wbcr_php_snippet id="24505"]
Veneers

Veneers are thin shells that are adhered to the surface of your teeth and are composed of porcelain. They are designed to conceal stains, cracks, misalignments, gaps, or other aesthetic imperfections. Veneers is a long-lasting solution for a perfect, natural-looking smile. Each veneer is customised for colour, shape and size to ensure a harmonious and natural appearance like your natural teeth.
Composite Bonding

Composite bonding is a contemporary technique for tooth repair that enables you to promptly fix chips, fissures and uneven surfaces. In order to replicate the natural tooth structure, a layer of composite resin is added and shaped during the process. In just one visit, the outcome is a polished and healthy appearance. This procedure is an ideal and affordable solution for minor imperfections.
Whitening Your Teeth

The goal of teeth whitening is to lighten enamel and get rid of stains from things like coffee, tea and tobacco. At KClinic we provide two options: at-home whitening under a dentist's supervision and in-clinic whitening for instant results. Both solutions provide a white, bright smile that lasts with the necessary maintenance and are safe and effective.
Smile Enhancement

A balanced and aesthetically beautiful smile is achieved by a combination of procedures known as smile enhancement. This can include tooth straightening, restoration of damaged areas or whitening. We provide a personalised treatment plan based on your functional and cosmetic requirements. To provide a smile that not only looks amazing but also supports dental health with our all-encompassing approach.

By Tube Our clinic is conveniently located near several London Underground stations, providing easy access from different parts of the city. The closest stations include Barbican, Farringdon, Old Street, St. Paul’s, and Angel, all within a short walking distance.

Barbican approximately 3 minutes' walk. Farringdon approximately 7–8 minutes' walk. Old Street approximately 8–10 minutes' walk. St. Paul's approximately 12 minutes' walk. Angel approximately 13 minutes' walk.

Numerous bus routes serve the area around our clinic, with key stops along Goswell Road and nearby Clerkenwell. Bus routes such as 4, 56, and 153 provide easy and reliable transport options from various parts of the city. imited on-street parking is available around Goswell Road, but restrictions may apply. Yes, a HydraGlowFacial is the ideal treatment to have before an important event.

minimizing areas of pigmentation.

How many sessions are required?

After the procedure, there may be some skin irritation or slight redness for a few hours, but these side effects normally go away in a few days.

Yes, IPL is frequently used in conjunction with other techniques including mesotherapy, chemical peels, and RF lifting. But it’s important to follow recommended intervals between treatments.

marks from stretching.
creases and fine lines.

title: FAQ General Dentistry
copy: FAQ General Dentistry

During check-up we thoroughly examine the oral cavity. This includes evaluating the teeth, gums and mucous membranes for indications of cavities, tartar, gum disease and other possible problems. If necessary, we use X-rays to diagnose other potential problems.
Yes, the checkup is completely painless. To ensure optimal comfort throughout the examination and diagnostics, our dentists work gently.

Yes, routine check-ups help in the identification of early cavities, even before they cause you some discomfort or other symptoms. Early diagnosis speeds up and improves the effectiveness of treatment teeth while preventing complications.

Our dentist will provide a detailed explanation and treatment plan if an issue is found during the check-up. If necessary, we will also assist in setting up a convenient time for any further procedures.
A check-up usually takes about 20-30 min. The duration may go up a little if more diagnostic tests, such X-rays, are required.
It doesn’t require any particular preparation. To maximise the effectiveness of the process, we advise you to just brush your teeth before the visit.
No, X-rays are only performed when necessary, such as if there are suspected hidden issues that cannot be seen during a standard examination.
Yes, check-ups are safe during pregnancy. But to avoid X-rays and other potentially dangerous procedures during this time, it is crucial to let the dentist know that you are pregnant.
Only an examination and diagnostics are part of the check-up. Although professional cleaning is a distinct process, we can combine them upon the patient’s request.
Yes, in order to guarantee appropriate care and avert possible problems, our dentists examine patients using braces and other orthodontic appliances.
We will try to provide an appointment the same day you get in touch with us. To guarantee a best time for your visit, we advise you to call us as soon as possible.
Serious toothache, facial or gum swelling, dental trauma, ongoing bleeding, and infection symptoms like fever or pus are symptoms that need to be treated right away.

Get in touch with us right away if a tooth is fractured or knocked out. Before your visit, we will provide you advice on what to do, schedule an urgent appointment to inspect the damage, and provide the necessary treatment.
Yes, we advise you to call us in advance to arrange your visit. This enables us to be ready fast and guarantee that you get the treatment you need.

Depending on the situation in your emergency appointments may include temporary fillings, infection treatment, tooth extractions, treatment of damaged teeth, pain management and other urgent procedures.
The complexity of your issue determines how long an emergency appointment will take. It could take anywhere from 30 to an hour on average.
Yes, in order to promote rapid healing and help avert future problems, our dentist will give you aftercare advice after the dental procedure.
Contact us if you’re not sure if you need an emergency appointment, and we’ll help you determine if you need to come in or not.
Modern anaesthetic techniques have made cavity treatment often painless. To guarantee optimal comfort throughout the procedure, our dentists can use local anaesthesia.
We make use of premium composite materials that complement the tooth’s natural colour. They have a visually good appearance and also are robust and long-lasting.

Cavities may be indicated if you detect dark patches on your teeth or feel sensitive or uncomfortable when chewing. Frequent checkups aid in the early signs of cavities and treat them before getting worse.
Composite fillings are long-lasting and can endure for 5-10 years, or even longer, depending on dietary and oral hygiene habits.
Fillings may erode or sustain harm over time. If you notice cracks or changes in the filling, we advise visiting a dentist for a check-up and potential replacement.
It’s important to maintain good dental hygiene after the treatment. To extend the life of the filling, brush your teeth twice a day, use dental floss and stay away from foods that are too hard.

Because composite fillings solidify under light, you can start eating right away after the procedure. But it is advised to stay away from extremely hot or cold foods for a few hours, this lessens sensitivity.
Modern filler materials rarely cause allergic responses. Make sure to let your dentist know about any known allergies or concerns before the procedure starts.

In most cases, getting your teeth cleaned by a professional is painless. Our dentists take everything to minimise any unpleasant feelings, however some patients may feel a little uncomfortable, particularly if there are sensitive areas or gum infection.
We advise getting professional dental hygiene every 6 months. But more regular cleaning may be necessary for patients who have a higher risk of gum disease or a propensity for plaque accumulation.
No, particular preparation is needed. In order for the dentist to effectively assess the state of your oral cavity, we advise brushing your teeth before your appointment.
Yes, professional dental hygiene can help get rid of surface stains from coffee, tea or other foods that leave stains.
Yes, professional cleaning leaves your mouth feeling clean and fresh by eliminating the microorganisms that cause foul breath.
After professional cleaning, fluoride treatment is an optional service that can be provided to strengthen enamel and prevent cavities, particularly for individuals who are more sensitive.
After professional cleaning, you can eat, but it’s best to wait for around half an hour, especially if fluoride was applied, to give it time to adhere to the teeth.
After cleaning, some patients may have momentary tooth sensitivity, especially if a large quantity of tartar was removed. Usually, this sensitivity goes away in a few days.

To preserve the effects of a professional dental cleaning for as long as possible, we advised to practise daily oral hygiene, which includes brushing your teeth twice a day, using dental floss and using mouthwash.

A root canal is a dental procedure used to treat an infection or inflammation inside the root of a tooth. To stop additional infection, the process entails cleaning, sealing, and removing any dead or infected pulp from the root canals.
The process is usually painless because of contemporary anaesthetic techniques. Compared to the agony of a tooth infection, you can experience some discomfort both during and after the procedure.
Depending on the case complexity, a root canal treatment can take from 60 to 90 minutes and is usually completed in one or more visits.
A crown is typically advised after root canal therapy to restore the tooth’s function and stop it from decaying.
Root canal therapy may not be necessary if cavities are found and treated early. Preventing serious harm requires maintaining good oral hygiene and scheduling every 6 month dental check-ups.
A tooth that has received root canal therapy can endure for many years, and in certain situations, forever, with the right maintenance.
If the tooth has significant damage and the pulp is infected, the only alternative may be tooth extraction. Root canal treatment is the only way to save the tooth.
No, other teeth are unaffected by root canal therapy. Only the afflicted tooth is the target of this therapy.
Yes, even after treating a root canal, a tooth may still be vulnerable to cavities if it is not taken care of properly. This emphasises how crucial it is to apply a crown for future protection.
After treating a root canal, the tooth may occasionally have a small colour change. For a more aesthetic appearance, we advise taking crown placement or teeth whitening.
There are two main types:
Simple Extraction: Performed on visible teeth under local anaesthesia.
Surgical Extraction: Required for teeth that are broken, impacted, or partially visible.

The dentist will use local anaesthetic to make the region numb. Sedation treatments might be available in difficult situations. In the event that stitches are required, the tooth will be carefully extracted.

Tooth extraction is usually painless thanks to current procedures and local anaesthetic. There shouldn’t be any serious discomfort, although you might feel some pressure. Prescription painkillers can be used to treat post-procedure discomfort.
Initial recovery often takes one to two weeks. It could take a little longer for the gum tissue to fully recover. Following aftercare instructions is key to quick and proper healing.
Bite into the gauze to stop the bleeding.
For the first 24 hours, stay away from hot or hard meals.
Apply cold compresses to lessen edema.
For a speedy recovery, adhere to the dentist’s aftercare instructions.

As with any procedure, tooth extraction may have certain risks, such as dry socket, infection or prolonged bleeding. However, these risks can be reduced by following your dentist’s aftercare instructions.

Because of their placement and potential to cause crowding or some difficulties, wisdom teeth frequently need to be surgically extracted. Our staff offers thorough aftercare instructions for a safe recovery, even though these extractions could take longer to heal.
In most cases, you can drive yourself home if only local anaesthetic is required. In the event that sedation was employed, you might require a companion.
For the first few hours, it is recommended to refrain from eating. You can eat soft foods and stay away from hot objects once the anaesthesia wears off.
Follow the dentist’s instructions for rinsing, use pain relievers as directed, and avoid touching the area with your tongue or fingers.

title: FAQ Aesthetic Dentistry
copy: FAQ Aesthetic Dentistry
When one or more teeth are lost, implantation is advised to restore both the smile’s appearance and chewing ability. Patients who would prefer not to wear detachable dentures can also use it.
Depending on the unique qualities of each patient, the osseointegration process can take anywhere from 3-6 months. The implant firmly fuses with the bone throughout this period.
The patient is not in pain because the procedure is carried out under local anaesthetic. Painkillers can be used to address any post-surgery discomfort.

Implant rejection and infection are among the risks associated with implantation, just like with any other surgical surgery. Nonetheless, these dangers are reduced by adhering to the dentist’s advice.
Yes, it is important to practise good oral hygiene, that includes brushing your teeth twice a day, using dental floss and routine check-ups with your dentist.
Better stability, a natural-toothlike appearance and feel, preservation of bone tissue, and minimal discomfort during eating or speaking are all benefits of implants.

Sufficient bone volume and the lack of significant surgical contraindications are prerequisites for dental implantation. A consultation with a dentist will help determine if you are a suitable candidate.
Dental implants can last a lifetime if properly maintained. They will remain in top shape with the support of routine dental examinations.
It is advised to avoid eating until complete sensitivity is restored after the operation. We advise eating soft foods for the first few days.
Following implant placement, it’s important to practise good oral hygiene, refrain from applying too much pressure to the implant, and schedule routine checkups with the dentist.
Yes, it is frequently possible to place several implants at once, which speeds up recuperation and saves time.
Yes, these help exact process planning, identification of possible problems and an accurate assessment of bone condition.
Depending on the case’s intricacy and the number of diagnostic tests required, the consultation usually lasts 30 to 60 minutes.
It is recommended to bring any relevant medical documentation, including a list of medications you are taking, and inform the dentist about any possible allergic reactions.
Any chronic diseases and medication you are taking should be disclosed to the dentist. This helps to account for potential risks and adjust the treatment plan accordingly.
Depending on the patient’s condition and whether further treatments are required, preparation may take a few days to several weeks.
Preparatory treatments and consultations are usually painless. X-rays and scans are examples of diagnostic procedures that don’t hurt.
Yes, our experts are available at all times to answer your concerns, offer assistance throughout the course of therapy, and thoroughly explain each stage of implantation.
The making of a crown usually takes one to two weeks. To save the tooth during this period, a temporary crown is affixed.

The patient is not in pain during the crown placement procedure because it is carried out under local anaesthesia. After the procedure you may feel minor discomfort, but this normally goes away in a few days.
We advise for front teeth ceramic or zirconia crowns due to their high aesthetics and natural appearance.

Yes, it is important to practise good oral hygiene, which includes using dental floss and brushing your teeth regularly. Maintaining the health of all your teeth and crowns also requires routine dental check-ups.
Although allergic responses to crown materials are extremely uncommon, make sure to let your dentist know if you have a metal allergy.

Despite being composed of sturdy materials, crowns are susceptible to chipping or cracking when subjected to extreme pressure or contact with extremely hard things. It is important to avoid biting hard items such as ice or nuts..
Crowns of superior quality usually hold their colour. However, it is recommended to limit the consumption of staining foods and drinks.
You should inform your dentist right once when the crown breaks or falls out for a future consultation and possible restoration.
Yes, crowns often advise to replace lost teeth on dental implants.
A crown is frequently advised after root canal therapy to preserve and repair the tooth.
The procedure is painless because carried out under anaesthetic. But after the treatment, there may be some minor discomfort, which usually goes away in a few days.

There are many types of dental bridges, such as conventional bridges, Maryland bridges, cantilever bridges and bridges supported by implants. Your dentist, based on your unique needs, will assist you in selecting the best course of action.
Yes, bridges are constructed from premium materials that are similar to natural teeth, it guarantees you a natural and beautiful teeth appearance.
From consultation to placement usually takes a few weeks, this depends on the complexity of the case and the dental lab’s schedule.
Get in touch with your dentist for an assessment and repair if your bridge becomes loose or damaged as soon as possible.
Yes, in order to maintain dental health and aesthetics, an old bridge can be replaced with a new one if it wears out or becomes broken.
Yes, other options can be removable partial dentures or dental implants. Based on your dental history and health, we can help you in choosing the best course of action.

High-quality bridge materials typically do not change their colour for many years. Nonetheless, maintaining their aesthetic appearance can be saved by routine cleaning and don’t eat foods that can discolour them.
Yes, bridges are popular and a stable and dependable way to restore multiple lost teeth in a succession.
Conventional dentures can be removed and they are affixed to the gums. While implant-supported dentures offer increased comfort and stability due to the implants.

The patient doesn’t experience any pain because the implantation process is carried out under local anaesthesia. But after that, there might be some minor discomfort, which normally goes away after a few days.
In order to give the gums time to relax and maintain good denture cleanliness, it is advised to take off dentures at night.
Implant-supported dentures have a far longer lifespan than conventional removable dentures – up to decades, when they are properly maintained.
Yes, dentures anchored by implants provide excellent stability, so you can consume a wide range of foods without worrying about them moving.
Although allergic responses to denture materials are uncommon, let your dentist know about any known allergies before the therapy.
The dentist evaluates each patient’s oral condition individually to choose the best denture option.
Depending on the patient’s needs and the condition of the dentures, removable dentures are usually updated every 5-7 years. But with good care they can last longer.
Because implant-supported dentures are firmly fixed to implants, there is very little chance that they will fall out or damage.
Depending on how well the implants integrate to the bone, the denture fixation and implant insertion process might take anywhere from a few months to half a year.
Over time, modifications or replacements can be required to preserve comfort and functionality, depending on the state of the oral cavity.

Depending on the complexity of the case and the state of the teeth, metal braces often last from one to three years. During the first consultation, orthodontists will determine your requirements and give you an expected treatment time.

Patients usually visit the orthodontist every four to six weeks for modifications. This guarantees constant pressure and tooth movement. The orthodontist modifies the archwire during these appointments to maintain the course of therapy.

It is advised to stay away from chewy, sticky and hard foods since they may cause harm to the braces or cause them to come loose or damaged. Also, you should stay away from items that might lead to plaque accumulation or get stuck between the braces.
Yes, the archwire is fastened using tiny elastic bands called ligatures, which come in a variety of colours. This enables you to choose your favourite colour or switch it up every time you come.
You should get in touch with your orthodontist immediately if a bracket or wire breaks. Also we recommend you plan your visit at once because even a short break may impact the course of therapy.

A retainer is used during the retention period that follows treatment completion. To keep the teeth from moving back, the retainer keeps them in their right place. The use of a retainer is a crucial step to secure the results.

Metal braces are suitable for most patients, regardless of age. However, based on your unique needs, the orthodontist will evaluate your teeth during your appointment and suggest the best course of action.
The danger of enamel damage is low if you practise good dental hygiene and care. Plaque accumulation around the brackets may be avoided by using floss and brushing your teeth on a regular basis.
To make adequate room for appropriate alignment, the orthodontist may occasionally advise removing one or more teeth. X-rays and an examination of your bite are used to make these decisions.
Yes, you can properly clean your teeth and braces with the use of specialised brushes, orthodontic floss and mouthwashes. The required hygiene supplies might be suggested by your orthodontist.
In order to maintain the outcomes attained, retainers are required. The retention period is a crucial component of therapy because without them, teeth can return to their initial positions.

Depending on the complexity of the case and the state of the teeth, ceramic braces usually last one to three years. During the consultation, the orthodontist will evaluate your dental condition and bite and offer a tentative treatment duration.
Although they aren’t completely clear, ceramic braces come in varieties that try to match the colour of the tooth as closely as possible. But compared to metal braces, they are far less obvious.
Yes, ceramic braces work just as well as metal braces at straightening teeth and fixing bite problems. They give the teeth the uniform pressure they need to achieve desired outcomes.
Elastic ligatures are used to secure the archwire, and they come in both transparent and coloured options. This allows you to change the appearance of your braces to suit your style.

Although the ceramic material itself is resistant to discoloration, some foods and beverages, such coffee, tea, or curry, can cause ligatures to change colour over time. Avoiding these meals or getting frequent ligature replacements are advised.
In certain situations, the orthodontist could suggest removing one or more teeth to make space for dental alignment. This choice is predicated on X-rays and a bite analysis.
Yes, using retainers after ceramic braces are taken off helps to save the results and keep teeth from moving back to their original positions, just as with any other kind of braces.
For the most case, the length of therapy for both types of braces is the same. But your orthodontist provides precise instructions on the best course of action for your circumstances.

Ceramic braces are totally safe for tooth enamel if orthodontic advice is followed and you oral hygiene is maintained. Enamel can be harmed by plaque accumulation from improper maintenance, but the braces themselves do not pose a risk.

Yes, some patients choose for metal braces on the lower jaw to save money and ceramic braces on the upper jaw for a less obvious appearance. Talk to your orthodontist about this option since it may not be appropriate in every situation.
Ceramic braces need to be handled carefully since they are more delicate than metal braces. The braces will stay in better shape if you stay away from sticky and firm meals.
The archwire may move when eating hard meals or moving teeth actively. If this happens it’s important to inform your orthodontist immediately for a correction.

Depending on the complexity case, the average duration of Aligners treatment is between 6 to 18 months. Based on your demands, your orthodontist will estimate the length of treatment during the first consultation.

Wearing aligners for 20 to 22 hours a day is recommended for best outcomes; they should only be taken out for eating, drinking (apart from water), and doing dental hygiene tasks like brushing and flossing.
Since Alighers aligners are composed of transparent plastic, they are practically invisible on your teeth. Most people won’t notice you’re wearing aligners, providing a discreet treatment option.

Many orthodontic problems, ranging from minor bite misalignment to more complicated situations, can be resolved with Aligners. But in really complicated cases, speaking with an orthodontist will help you decide if Alighers is suitable for you.

When eating or drinking anything other than water, aligners should be taken out to avoid damage or discoloration, if this is not done the food particles being stuck between the aligner and your teeth.

Rinse your aligners with warm water and, if necessary, use a gentle brush to clean. Additionally, Alighers provides specialised cleaning solutions to preserve the hygiene and clarity of the aligners. But avoid hot water since it might cause the aligners to deform.

For the first few days after beginning a new set of aligners, some patients could feel a little pressure or pain. This is typical while your teeth are being moved by the aligners. Usually, the discomfort goes away in a few days.

Contact with your orthodontist immediately if you misplace or break an aligner. Until a replacement aligner becomes available, they could advise you wearing the prior aligner or going to the next set.
It is not advised during treatment because whitening might cause uneven tooth colour. For an even outcome, we recommended whitening after treatment.
It is feasible to transition from braces to Alighers in some situations. To find out if you can do this transition for your particular treatment plan, speak with your orthodontist.

Within a few weeks, some patients experience slight changes but the visible outcomes are contingent upon the duration of the treatment plan and regular aligner usage. The final result and anticipated improvement will be discussed with you by your orthodontist.
Yes, it’s essential to have a case to store your aligners when you remove them for eating or oral care. This helps keep them clean and protects them from loss or damage.

Yes, the majority of people adjust to retainers rather fast. While detachable retainers are simple to take off and put back on, making eating and hygiene easier, permanent retainers are undetectable and do not obstruct speaking.

Because permanent retainers are affixed to the inner surfaces of the teeth, they are imperceptible. Removable retainers are nearly undetectable on teeth because they are composed of transparent plastic.
Long-term retainers: To avoid plaque accumulation, it’s important to use dental floss or interdental brushes to clean the teeth around the wire on a regular basis.
Detachable retainers: After removal, gently brush retainers without toothpaste, use only warm water and preserve in a safe container.

Permanent retainers can shatter or separate from the teeth, requiring repair. Removable retainers should be maintained in a case and away from hot water since they can distort in hot temperatures or break if improperly stored.
Every 6-12 months, we recommend visiting your orthodontist for a retainer check-up. This makes it possible to identify any problems and fix them in order to preserve consistent outcomes.

Inform your orthodontist immediately if a permanent retainer breaks or a detachable retainer disappears. Wearing a retainer is essential to maintaining treatment results, so replacement or repair should be done promptly.
When you first start wearing a retainer, some minor discomfort is expected, but any pain should go away in a few days. See your orthodontist if it continues.
Yes, after a few years, removable retainers may need to be replaced due to wear and tear. Permanent retainers can also need to be replaced if they deteriorate or break.
Yes, you should inform your orthodontist right away to set up a replacement if you lose your retainer.
Without a retainer, teeth can start moving in a matter of days or weeks, so it’s critical to wear it consistently and adhere to your orthodontist’s recommendations.
The veneer placement procedure is rather painless because before the procedure we use anaesthetic. Although there may be some little pain, most people find the process to be comfortable.

Veneers typically take 2-3 dentist appointments for installation. Consultation and preparation take place during the first appointment, impressions are taken during the second and the veneers are finally placed during the third.
Veneers are permanent and cannot be taken out. But when they are broken, the dentist can remove them. However, depending on care and tooth health, they may need replacement after 10-15 years.
Yes, if veneers are exposed to extremely hard foods or excessive force, they may crack or shatter. See your dentist about replacing or repairing a broken veneer.
Because porcelain is so stain-resistant, veneers will stay coloured for a lot longer than natural enamel—as long as they are properly maintained and exposed to less staining agents.
Most individuals can have veneers, but if you have serious dental problems like gum inflammation or widespread tooth decay, your dentist could suggest alternative treatments first.
In order to create a more natural look and beautiful smile, porcelain veneers can indeed assist bridge small gaps between teeth.
Since porcelain cannot be whitened, it is crucial to take care of it properly and stay away from staining agents. If colour change is necessary over time, the veneers will need to be replaced.
In order to enhance the appearance of a smile, porcelain veneers are usually applied to the front teeth. If required, they can occasionally be positioned on side teeth.
After veneers are installed, there may be a little sensitivity to hot or cold meals, but this normally goes away in a few days.
Although veneers are often not applied to teeth that have crowns, your dentist may recommend other cosmetic restoration techniques.

In most cases, composite bonding is painless. Since the technique only requires minimum tooth surface preparation, anaesthesia is not necessary. To provide comfort, a mild anaesthetic may be used if significant reshaping is required.
Composite bonding works well, but it is not as strong as porcelain veneers and can crack or discolour easily. Over time, it might need to be replaced or touch-up sometimes.

Yes, composite resin is more prone to discoloration. Coffee, tea, red wine, smoking and other staining agents can cause bonding to discolour over time. Discoloration can be avoided by practising good oral hygiene and using staining agents sparingly.
Unlike real teeth, composite bonding is not affected by teeth whitening. Your dentist can update or replace the bonding if the material’s colour changes over time.
Yes, small damage to composite bonding may be fixed. If necessary, the dentist might replace the composite entirely or apply a fresh coating on some teeth.

Because technique necessitates only minor alterations to the tooth structure, composite bonding is indeed reversible. The composite may be taken out if needed without causing any harm to the tooth below.
Composite bonding is suitable for closing small gaps and making minor cosmetic corrections. Orthodontics or other restorative techniques may be necessary for significant gaps or alignment problems.

Bonding is usually employed for front teeth since the composite material may not be able to tolerate strong chewing pressures. It can, however, occasionally be used on molars for little fixes or cosmetic adjustments.

Because of the additional layers, the treated tooth may feel a little different after bonding. But in a few days the bonding feels like a natural part of the tooth and most patients quickly get used to it.
Yes, veneers can be used in place of composite bonding if preferred. Depending on your cosmetic preferences and the state of your teeth, your dentist can go over your alternatives.
Usually, teeth whitening procedures are painful. Temporary tooth sensitivity may occur in certain patients, although it usually goes away soon.
Depending on lifestyle choices, dental cleanliness, and following the dentist’s advice, results can last anywhere from six months to two years.
No, whitening solutions do not alter the colour crowns or fillings; they only operate on natural enamel.

It is advised to abstain from tobacco and staining goods (tea, coffee, red wine, and berries) for 48 hours following in-office teeth whitening. These limitations are in effect for the duration of at-home teeth whitening.
Yes, both techniques are safe when used under a dentist’s supervision. Depending on the state of your gums and teeth, the dentist modifies the concentration of the gel.
Reduce how long you wear the trays or see your dentist if your sensitivity worsens. They might suggest a lower-concentration gel or a toothpaste designed to reduce sensitivity.
Yes, the dentist keeps an eye on things and modifies the treatment plan as necessary.
Yes, you can repeat the procedure, but only as often as your dentist recommends or no more than once a year.
Professional in-office whitening is more effective for such cases. If results are insufficient, the dentist may recommend alternative treatments like veneers.
Before the whitening procedure a professional cleaning is advised, it improves the penetration of the whitening gel by removing tartar and plaque.
To verify that the trays fit properly, get in touch with your dentist. Trays created to order should fit pleasantly and snugly.
In-office whitening can lighten teeth by 5–10 shades, while at-home whitening can achieve 4–8 shades depending on the condition of the enamel and the method used.

Smile enhancement is a series of dental procedures intended to improve your teeth’s appearance, functionality and general health. It could involve veneers, implants, tooth whitening, straightening, or repairing broken teeth.
We employ local anaesthesia to guarantee your comfort during the treatments. Rarely, some clients could feel some minor discomfort, but it usually goes away in a few days.
The quantity and kind of procedures determine the timeline:
Teeth whitening takes 1–2 visits.
Veneer placement may take 1–2 weeks.
Orthodontic treatment with aligners lasts 6 months to 1.5 years.
Yes, we provide wax mock-ups or digital modelling so you may see how your smile will look in the future and make any necessary alterations before treatment starts.
Yes, before certain procedures, a professional teeth cleaning or treatment for gum disease and cavities may be necessary.
Yes, a number of therapies, like orthodontic therapy or implants, improve general dental health by restoring teeth’s functionality in addition to improving their appearance.
Yes, in order to get the best outcomes in the shortest period of time, we can integrate many treatments into a one treatment plan.
Yes, a lot of patients decide to improve their smiles before important events like weddings or photoshoots.
We will create you a customised treatment plan that will provide you with the smile of your dreams if you schedule a consultation at our facility.

“I’ve struggled with rosacea for years and nothing ever worked. After just a few treatments here, the redness has gone down so much, and my skin feels calmer than ever. Thank you!”

Sophie L.
“My skin always irritated because of my rosacea, but after these treatments, it feels normal again. The redness is almost gone, and I couldn’t be happier.”

Jessica P.
“I had some acne scars and uneven texture, but after the Laser Skin Resurfacing, my skin looks amazing. The results are even better than I expected. Highly recommend!”
Charlotte S.
“I’m so happy I tried this treatment. My skin feels softer, and the lines around my eyes have almost disappeared. It’s honestly the best thing I’ve done for my skin!”
Olivia W.
“Laser Skin Resurfacing has completely transformed my skin. My scars are barely visible now, and the overall texture is so much smoother. I can’t stop looking in the mirror!”

“The whole experience was fantastic. The team explained everything clearly, and the results are incredible. My skin has never felt or looked this good!”
